lines.x: Hello. You've reached the office of Rhine Lab Control, Kristen Wright.
Ms. Wright is currently unable to take your call. Please leave your message after the tone. Thank you.
This is bad, Kristen. I think I screwed up...
I'll admit the Colonel's smart. He caught onto my 'little tricks' last time I made a delivery.
He's been all eyes on me along with Ferdinand's people. It took a whole bunch of clones just to get away here.
Ferdinand's gonna make his move at Rhine soon enough, I just know it... and the other Directors have to fall in line. The only other option is to play dumb.
Ugh, jeez. I might have nowhere to run anymore, seriously.
I'm a little scared. Just a tiny, tiny bit, but... you get where I'm coming from, right?
Not to worry, though. I'm still going to uphold our agreement, 100%.
—Kristen... I can't help but wonder...
If she was still here, maybe we wouldn't...
Ms. Muelsyse, I suggest you stop struggling.
Your marathon from Rhine HQ to the commercial district's wasted our entire evening.
...*Sigh*.
Lemme guessie, dead end for me?
You'll always catch me as long as I'm somewhere in Trimounts.
No. Forget Trimounts. It'd be the same in any city.
This is Columbia. The charges stick to lawbreakers, rain or shine.
This is about me breaking confidentiality with the military, right?
In which case, you should give me some time to call my lawyer—and not sic a kajillion armed mercs on me in the middle of the street.
I'll say again. We're in Columbia.
We don't operate by the books unless 'by the books' is what we need. In other scenarios, we like efficiency.
Alright. Do I still get a little chat with Ferdinand? Maybe me and him can still straighten out this whole mix-up before it's too late.
Nuh-uh. That's not in my orders here.
D... Didn't Ferdinand hire you?
Ferdinand Clooney might be your coworker, but he's just a partner of convenience to me.
I've got no personal stake in the Rhine infighting. Ecological? Energy? I don't give a damn and my employer doesn't care who exactly we're cooperating with.
What we want is results. Rhine Lab provides the goods, my employer puts thought into their use.
And your tiny little 'oops moment' lost you the last ounces of our trust.
If I said... I didn't mean to, would you believe me?
I'm a researcher, ya know? I've got this habit of hanging onto test data here and there.
Habit? You had all this time to think of an excuse, and that's it?
I'm so, so sorry to tell you, your fancy brand of charisma doesn't work on me.
I'm given to understand Ferdinand warned you not to let a whisper of any of the testing leak before the government passed every bill tied to it.
Your Elven blood's stretched the expiration date on your good looks. That likewise ensures you don't form any forgetful habits, right?
You—!
You know I'm...
An Elf?
I read the files. I know who I'm working with. Part of my job, y'see.
So, come, let's cooperate some. Whatever illusion you were going to concoct next, it'd be as useless as the last.
Sheesh, I guess I really am cornered.
I guess that's why you were stalling so long... You think you can dupe me?
I'm not the brute forcing type, I don't go off half-cocked, and I'm not one of your colleagues married to the lab.
Got you.
*cough* *cough*...
At this temperature... water molecules... don't hear my call...
Is this your Arts... or some kind of special technology?
Are you really free enough to discuss issues of science with me, 'Director' Muelsyse?
Sorry, but I'm tired of playing hide-and-seek, and I don't want any sudden 'acts' of iced water getting my plumage or hair wet.
Hahah... you're... no ordinary merc...
Flattered.
Now, you still have one last chance—answer my question.
Kristen Wright, Rhine Lab Control, has spent the past several years buying up a massive amount of high-purity hard material from companies high and low. What kind of experiment is she running?
Hfff... hkaah... why are you helping Ferdinand if you and your employer—care so much about Control's tests?
That's not the right answer. That's not AN answer. That's another question, in fact.
Nngh...
I don't have... any of... the answers you're looking for.
Your file says you love using your blood-given abilities to toy with people. I'm a little surprised you turned out to be so loyal to the Control.
Loyal...?
I'm just... looking out for... myself...
Aww... you're telling me you don't want this chance at all.
In which case, Ms. Muelsyse—
Our partnership is hereby terminated.
Terminated...?
Terminate her.
Saria...
You've gotta make it in time... for Kristen...
2:43 A.M. \ Overcast
Main District of Trimounts
Still waiting for your appointment, ma'am?
She should've been here ten minutes ago.
I've never counted on her to be punctual, but if our last comms were anything to go by, she was the one desperate for this meeting.
Maybe she backed out again...
We all know the Director of Rhine Lab Ecological prefers to hide the truth behind all those smiles of hers.
I don't trust Muelsyse.
I wouldn't easily trust anyone choosing to remain at Rhine Lab at this point.
But partnerships are built on more than just trust. Did you trust me the moment I first met you?
*cough*... The truth, ma'am?
When I saw you standing outside my door, I almost went straight to the kitchen to grab a knife or something, and call the cops along the way.
Good head on your shoulders.
Still, Rhine Lab wouldn't use force to threaten a retired employee who signed an NDA.
At least, not while I was still in Defense.
A new message?
Something wrong, ma'am? You look serious... serious enough to give me shivers.
Change of plans.
Muelsyse isn't coming.
S... Something happened to her? Right when she was about to meet you?
Erikson, I need a favor.
W-What is it?
Send the info I received to the local branch of this pharmaceutical company.
Ask their operators to immediately inform Dr. Kal'tsit and Dr. Doctor—I know they've been in Columbian territory recently.
I need their help.
5:11 A.M. \ Overcast
Test Site 359, Trimounts Outskirts
Morning, Doctor. You're up as early as ever.

opts: Well-regulated circadian rhythm promotes stability of core data.

lines.x: D... Data? You mean physiologically, right?
Haha, you always pack that special sense of humor.
*sigh*... I pulled an all-nighter just to get these figures. Director Franks is gonna rag on me for this.

opts: Negative. Scenario inconsistent with typical behavior of Dorothy Franks.

lines.x: Uh, is that supposed to reassure me?
Thanks... but even if she lets it fly, there's still Director Clooney, and I don't wanna go back to him...
You're overdue for a good night's sleep, Helen.
Elena... you know we have evaluations next week. How am I supposed to sleep with an eval weighing on me?
I can't lose this job. I'm still paying off my student loans...
Sorry, I know I keep bringing that up.
Hm... how's this sound? I'll raise your situation with the Director once the experiment's over.
S-Seriously? Elena... you're the only one in all of Energy who isn't terrified of Director Clooney. What's your secret?
You've just gotta be like me...
Spending every single day in the lab, making the Director happy with every last thing you do? Th—That's not realistic for me...
Oh, right! I needed to check in with Director Franks! Gotta go!
See you.
I made coffee. You want some?

opts: Excessive caffeine intake may aggravate Oripathy, Elena.

lines.x: I hear you, but I'm still not about to give up my life for the word 'may.'
If the docs had the final word, I'd be best off staying at Rhodes Island with my sister.
Same for you, right, Joyce? Medical wants you aboard long-term.

opts: Regulations state I must report back to Rhine Lab at regular intervals.
Provision of medical services at this site is only a temporary assignment.

lines.x: Sure... just a few days, and then you'll meet back up with Dr. Silence in the city, right?
Me and Dorothy'll miss you.
Joyce needs rest. Work gets too intense here at the site.
And you, Elena. If it wasn't for Ferdinand talking you up as his most outstanding researcher, I'd be on the case to kick you back to the city too.
So you're here at last, Dorothy.
Helen just went looking for you. You didn't run into her?
Sorry, I wasn't paying attention...
Seriously, you're gonna work until you faint, huh?
Forget me and Joyce, how many days have you gone without sleep? I've seen the wrappers filling up your lab's trash can over the past week. Agrypnotics, huh?
The experiment's already in its final phase. I promised the participants I'd accelerate the pace, so they can get back to their families sooner.
Got me there. Who doesn't want this done sooner?
Plus, as your assistant, I have to listen to you anyway.
Alright—can I ask my beloved assistant to do me another little favor, then?
I can't be out of the lab for too long. I want you to courier these heat-emitter modules to some friends outside.
Heat-emitters? We've been in the barrens for over a month. Where'd you buy these?

opts: Warning. Rhine Lab technological components detected.

lines.x: See? It took me and Joyce one look to know you made these with lab materials.
The living fossils up in Business won't like that. You might be Director of Originium Arts Director, but that's not gonna change their tune any.
I know how their minds work.
And I know even better that you won't report something this minor to Ferdinand, or any of the Directors for that matter.
They need these things. It's been getting cold lately, and their huts weren't built to last. They won't keep the chills out.
Dorothy.
I've been wanting to say... Don't you think you worry too much about them?
They're... Pioneers. A team of them in Rhine Lab's employ.
No Pioneer's gonna hang around anywhere for long, let alone make friends.
They're just a bunch of kids.
Besides, I know what you're trying to say. You're worried about me. You're worried I'll feel empty once they're gone.
That's my little assistant, always so considerate...
Don't grin at me like that. You're not my teacher, Director Franks.
Elena...
Fine, I'll do you this one last favor. The very last one.
Seeing as... the experiment's nearly over.
Sir, we're all set.
Sonny... Sir?
Huh?
Got the heebie-jeebies?
What? Nah, that's never been my style.
You've been pacing laps about the cabin. Seven, coming up eight.
I'm just, uh... I'm getting my mind in order. Lotta variables, what's coming up next.
Sonny, I've been thinking, but, uh, are we really on the right track here?
Them scientists, and the doc, they've been plenty good to us, I say.
I told y'all already, one or two acts of kindness don't make a saint.
Back in law school, my classmates loved to all that charitable business—
You know, donate a thing or three to treatment zones for Infected, maybe join a campus debate for justice, for what they went through.
But once I came to be an unlucky, ill-ridden son-of-a-gun too, well, guess how many times they visited me?
Yeah?
Not a single goddamned time.
A year back, I went to borrow some money from an old college roommate. He worked at the biggest law firm in Max D.C.
Picked up the phone, get this. He asked me who I was.
'Sorry, you were out deferred for treatment, so that's why the grad yearbook didn't have your contact.' —Ha!
Most touching joke I ever heard, I tell you.
Real band of bastards, huh? We've seen plenty of 'em ourselves, all of us.
Once they're at a safe enough distance, it stokes their egos good to throw out a little benevolence, like they're feeding fowl, and it makes a topic for their get-togethers too.
You go on and guess... if right this moment, in that laboratory, those respectable scientists are cradling their coffee cups talking about us, huh?
Nobody minds doling out good intent, not until it costs them anything for real.
Alright, boss, whatever you say.
You seen the most of us, paid it most mind. Your word is gold.
This is the only way we've got.
Nobody in the whole damned test site's gonna look out for us, 'cept ourselves.
C'mon, Joyce, once I finish my coffee, we've got to get to work.
Medic... we need a medic!
You're one of the Pioneers?
You can't really just come in... uh, you haven't gone through decontam yet...
Don't you have a medic or a doctor? Something came over Sam all of a sudden, he's sick!
Sick...? Is it Oripathy?!
Are you? Are you a doctor? You gotta save him, you gotta save ol' Sam, save us all!
He's been running like a kettle since late last night, and he's just started throwing up blood!

opts: Please input the patient's location.

lines.x: He's in the nearest hut... We did like you told us last time, gave him some painkillers, laid him down.

opts: Understood. Initiating emergency treatment routine.

lines.x: Joyce...!

opts: Apologies. Other tasks have reduced priority.

lines.x: I... I know.
I'm not trying to stop you, even if we're shattering safety regulations...
Joyce, I'm gonna go with you.

opts: You are not a medical worker, Elena.

lines.x: Who gives two cares? Remember, I took basic medical training too. An extra pair of helping hands can't hurt in a situation like this, right?
Plus I've gotta make the trip anyway, with my errand from Dorothy. Might as well be now.
Here!
Where's the patient?
Out of the way! Every second counts with acute Oripathy onset!
But...

opts: Scan returned no results.

lines.x: Don't move!
Is that... a nail gun?

opts: There are no cases of acute Oripathy onset in the vicinity.

lines.x: Sure aren't, Joyce. These are some healthy young men here.
Wouldn't have the strength to be pointing dangerous tools at us otherwise.
Hands up, ladies.
You're... the leader of the Pioneer Team.
You remember me? I admit I'm a touch surprised.
Sir, why have you tricked me and Joyce into coming here?
I trust you know, Urbica... Dr. Urbica.
That's how you say your surname, right?
You pronounced it beautifully...
Gonna trouble you to lend us your communicator there, Doc.
Pioneer Team needs to ask your superiors a few questions.
3:56 P.M. \ Overcast
Main District of Trimounts
This is the third time you've paged through Ptilopsis's personal log, Doctor.
Any new discoveries?

opts: Ptilopsis keeps some detail-rich records.
Pioneer Team leader had a good education.
Elena's coffee ought to be pretty good.

lines.x: Ptilopsis has a kind of special chip implanted in her brain.
'Device #9'—Mayer calls it. Guessing that's Rhine Lab's internal codename for that tech.
It's plugged up there directly into her nervous system. Functions as a regular part of her mind, and logs data on neural activity in each region and lobe all the while.
Which means you've just been looking at every last thing Ptilopsis's visual processors—her own two eyes—have been seeing.
Columbia's Pioneer Team members come from all backgrounds.
Youth dissatisfied with their lives, middle-aged couples drowning in debt, petty criminals who don't want to end up behind bars—
And of course, Infected powerless to cough up exorbitant insurance, who don't resign themselves to being shut away in quarantine zones for 'treatment.'
I'm more interested in the agrypnotic Franks has been using, personally.
I love a drink after work as much as the next, but all that fluid intake on the job's bound to cut up your train of thought.
From the logs Ptilopsis keeps synchronized with us, we can tell that along with her, there's our other Rhodes Island-partnered operator, Elena in Rhine Lab's Energy Section...
Along with Rhine Lab Originium Art Section's Director, Dorothy Franks. That's everyone caught up in this abduction by the Pioneers.
And practically simultaneously, Saria informed us Rhine Lab Ecological Section's Director Muelsyse went missing after she sent out an SOS, and there's no telling if she's currently alive or dead.

opts: Think there's an issue at Rhine Lab?
Are research jobs seriously this high-risk?

lines.x: At present, looks like it.
Welcome to Columbia.
Sky-high odds Rhine Lab is already in crisis, if things are pushing Saria to ask Rhodes Island for help.
Lucky the landship's nearby, or else we'd be hard-pressed to make an expeditious response.
The kidnapping's gone down at a new site about a hundred or so klicks out from Trimounts, but Kal'tsit's concerned this has something to do with the experiments that got shut down a while back.

opts: What do they do at that site?
Originium mutation experimentation...
Tell me some more about Trimounts.

lines.x: The stuff they show to outsiders indicates it's your typical testing grounds, currently in the middle of construction.
Kal'tsit's cleared us to access all relevant files before we enter Trimounts.
The city's the beating heart of Columbian science and technology. Hundreds of tech companies are headquartered in Trimounts.
The 'Diαbolic Crisis,' 'Loken Watertank,' the 'Lumberyard.'
Each name means something to one of our Rhodes Island operators, and behind their fronts, ties to hundreds of similar experiments.
Most of these experiments have involved Originium's mutative effects on living beings' cells, and each one has been incredibly dangerous.
In Columbia, more often than not, tech giants like Rhine Lab aren't going to participate directly in such high-risk experiments.
They'll have smaller-scale organizations carry them out on their behalf, hire a Pioneer Team, construct a suitable site out in the wasteland fringes of the city to execute their experimental work.
In this case, Ptilopsis is at Site 359, which is one of those.

opts: I remember your previous work experience...
Have you done work at similar sites before?

lines.x: Work? I'm not gracing my time there with the word 'work.'
Those megacorps 'plagiarized' what I created, and applied it all in places it wasn't meant to be.
See that billboard, about fifty meters out? 'Volvort Kochinski's Automatic Popcorn-a-Tron for the home—faster, sweeter, every time.'
I still have the prototype sketch for its energy core on my terminal.
It was supposed to be used in automatic wasteland road-cleaners at first, but my manager decided it'd never sell as a product.
He says, what kind of HR company's spending on better equipment for Pioneers?
Brought my mind to Rhodes Island after that. Our course-clearer team in Engineering's using the very latest model.
I've long seen it coming. Be it Volvort Kochinski or Rhine Lab, in the end, all these so-called tech firms are going to be destroyed by their own creations—
Because they've never respected the creations themselves.

opts: What if Muelsyse got killed by her very own experiments?
It's not some horrific test subject holding Ptilopsis hostage, right?!

lines.x: Was that a laugh line, Doctor?
Because what you said isn't sci-fi flick material, or a dumb joke.
Chances are it's tomorrow's news.

opts: Now I get why Closure admires AND fears you...
No wonder you and Logos make a good team.

lines.x: You mean her occasional stints as target practice for my new weapons?
She can stop interrupting my work with her ridiculous ideas, and then she won't need to fear me.
As a partner, Logos is pretty good.
But can you give him a reminder, Doctor?
If he could cut some of his speechcraft, mission times would go down by at least 30%, and I could spend more time on my actual job.
Done processing the intel, Doctor.
This is as much useful info as we have for now. I've already set the most important files to top priority on lookup. You can read them via PRTS at any time.

opts: Any more news?

lines.x: Ptilopsis's logs haven't been updated past this point.
Device #9's data transmission should be more stable ever since Mayer adjusted it.
So unless something physically happened to Ptilopsis...

opts: I'm getting concerned for her.
Fingers crossed Silence and Greyy find her ASAP.

lines.x: Silence will notify us of any developments at the site as they happen.
Saria's copied the info. She'll be rendezvousing with us in an hour.
Doctor, any other proposals for the time we have left?

opts: Go to where Muelsyse hit trouble, take a look.

lines.x: I'm a mechanist. I'm no detective's assistant.

opts: But it's all we can do right now.
And I'm just an Originium researcher.
You're the only one I can count on, Mechanist.

lines.x: Alright, Doctor. In that case, we move now.
Here's hoping I'm back on the landship by tomorrow, at my beloved workbench.
6:47 P.M. \ Overcast
Test Site 359 Observation Station, Trimounts Outskirts
Anything new with the people on-site?
Nothing, officer.
You shot word to your Director Clooney or whatever at Rhine, right? When exactly will he be here?
Director Clooney's in a... meeting right now.
Meeting? You're telling me he's still in a meeting? What kind of meeting's more important than his employees' lives?
Dammit!
Give me those binoculars.
Uh... okay.
You don't think those Pioneers are gonna follow through, do you?
Let me tell you—they will.
Push them enough, and they'll do any fricking thing.
So you mean our colleagues at the site could be in mortal danger?
Who are you?
Olivia Silence, Rhine Lab researcher, and Rhodes Island Pharmaceuticals-partnered Medic Operator.
This is my colleague from Rhodes Island, Mr. Greyy with the Engineering Department.
H-Hello! I'm a Rhodes Island electrical engineer... but I'm still interning!
Rhodes Island? What kind of useless crap does that company do?
Er...
Forget it, save the explanation. One day passes in Trimounts and a barge of fresh company names unloads out of the sewers.
You're Rhine Lab, right? Got any pull in the office?
Pull? Ma'am, I'm not too sure what you mean...
Forget it, I knew you wouldn't be in charge.
You strike me as one of those... eggheads-swimming-in-books types, like the hostages they've got.
You guys and those guys might as well live on different planets.
I heard they were the Pioneers working at this site...
Pioneers.
The name rings too nice for anything except billboards.
And I know better than anyone what kind of people they are. Convicts, gamblers, Infected who can't stick the medical fees... Nothing to lose by the time they went out to the wastes.
Excuse me... could we try and talk to them?
You might have realized already, but Dr. Silence and I are both Infected. We're in a place to understand their situation.
Rhine Lab makes stupid cash, doesn't it? So does Rhodes Island or whatever?
You've got these, let's say 'well-intentioned,' companies paying your insurance premiums—that's the key thing.
That's why you can shut up about understanding them.
Premiums? Which part exactly is...
It's the way things work here in Columbia. A policy aimed at Infected.
Ma'am, please trust us—Rhodes Island is a pharmaceutical corporation dedicated to addressing Infected issues.
Addressing issues? If I gave you a crossbow and told you to square away those kidnappers from here, would you do it?
...I couldn't.
I'm a doctor.
What about him?
Me? N-No, I don't think we've reached the point where we have to resort to violence...
Then it looks to me like you two can't help much at all.
No sign of anyone coming, sir.
They're playing it cool.
Say, you think the megacorps might not, uh... care if their hires die?
What with those two being Infected and all.
Don't give me that.
Infected aren't subhuman or nothing. At the very least, we don't wanna treat each other like that.
Alright... said that on the Pioneer recruitment ads too.
Well, count our lucky stars the ads ain't all lies, then.
Would you kindly drop the communicator, Dr. Urbica?
He said drop it! No funny business!
Alright.
I'm... I'm putting it down. Please don't hurt us.
Sir, she was sending messages!
Bring me those comms, Sam.
Hmm. Only half-typed. Didn't get to send anything yet.
These are our exact coordinates. You want them to send a drone in or something?
Let me give you another idea. Change this to an SOS, send it to your tutor, your manager... whoever.
Tell them—I'm waiting. And my patience is running out.
What exactly are you after?
Well that's wicked simple, doctor.
Me and my friends here, we all just wanna leave this place alive. That's all.
Director, finally, you're back! Can you spare a moment?
You're... Ben. Ben from Structural.
Y-You remember my name?
Call me Ferdinand. Nine whole directors at Rhine Lab and all.
Sir... Ferdinand.
My next meeting is in ten minutes.
I can spare you seven of those.
Ahh... alright, thank you, thank you.
Sir, do you know where Control is right now?
I've got Structural's application for equipment purchases here. Control was supposed to give us an answer three days ago... but we haven't heard anything from her.
Structural's having to suspend a lot of its experiments right now...
Give me the form.
Alright, go procure.
Did... Did you just sign? The handbook says large-scale equipment purchases this expensive have to go through Control...
How long have you been with Rhine Lab? Just a couple of months?
Yes, sir... I reported in a month ago.
See this site in front of you?
Huh?
Isn't it monumental?
Fifteen years ago, this place was still wasteland, not even a single nomadic plate yet.
You wouldn't see Rhine Lab on a foldout of Trimounts back in those days.
Meet Parvis for tea, if get the chance.
You've got a lot to learn, Ben. And you won't find it in your handbook.
A-Alright... Got it, sir.
Well look at that, I still have four minutes left.
Not often you call me when it's light out, Ferdinand.
Ho'olheyak.
Are you done with everything I asked?
Mmhm... at least three empty seats for Rhine Lab's directors' meeting today?
No. That's not enough.
Oh? Which director's next?
Are you finally fed up with the old goat behind the chimeric organism experiments, or do you want the dreamer at the test site to never come back?
Word is Rhodes Island's people went to the site.
The experiment's almost complete. I don't want anyone getting in the way before it's done, understood?
You want me on site?
No, no need for that.
How cruel. Your assistant's one of the hostages, isn't she? I saw that Elena girl once. She's as pretty as she is hardworking.
I had a feeling you'd like her.
She's with Energy. She can wrangle the Pioneers.
You keep an eye on developments in the city.
It's unlikely Rhodes Island would only send two operators to the site. Don't forget, Saria's had a line to them all this time.
Oh? Your Defense Director again?
Saria's already stalled our experiments long enough... Both the Colonel and I are running short of patience.
Remember, they can't find anything this time.
This is the place, Doctor.
The last message we got from Saria was sent from this alley.

opts: Looks normal to me.
We aren't exactly near Rhine Lab HQ.
Right by the commercial sector.

lines.x: There's hundreds of these alleys in Trimounts.
Here to there is about a half-hour trip by car.
That's the exact sector she was in.
She was waiting for Muelsyse. They'd agreed to meet at a specific time.

opts: Seems like Muelsyse got chased here.

lines.x: If she wasn't being pursued, she'd never choose to set foot in this alley.
No matter who it was, acting in the open streets would've obviously been too risky.

opts: Silence told me about Muelsyse's ability.
Saria gave me a report on Muelsyse once.

lines.x: Right... search complete.
'Ability to manipulate water molecules'—would that be energy conversion-type or matter restructuring-type in terms of Originium Arts? No matter which principle it is, it's powerful at a glance.
'Water molecules'—no flaws visible to the naked eye?
Even more interesting. Experimental research folks and us mechanists are alike. Our powers of observation are usually razor-keen.
If it fooled even Silence and Mayer, then it's not your ordinary Arts illusion.

opts: Muelsyse wouldn't go down easy in a fight.
Muelsyse has strong self-preservation abilities.
Muelsyse is basically fight and flight in one.

lines.x: There's a couple of scorch marks on the ground.
Not very wide-range... whoever did it, all signs point to some kind of technical equipment used to heat the air surrounding Muelsyse.
Considering her Arts are heavily dependent on water molecules, her opponent must've known her very well.

opts: As well as we do.

lines.x: You mean...
They might be a Rhine insider too?
Doctor, watch out!

opts: That was close...
I was almost well-done there.

lines.x: That was an energy weapon.
Shot from deep inside the alley. Enemy's only thirty meters from us, give or take.
And getting closer.
Doctor, you'll want to stay behind me.
Defense system active.

opts: Think we're in an ambush?
Can we hold out?

lines.x: Dr. Doctor, I've never seen this model of power armor before. It's new.
Without testing, I can't tell you exactly how destructive any weapon hooked up to this could get.
And by that, I mean we need a change of plans.
Escape system active—now get on my back!
I... can't believe it.
I had signal interference active and everything. Even Closure's own latest drone gen wouldn't detect our location.
What sensory tech is this power armor using?

opts: Can you paralyze it?

lines.x: I don't want to draw attention...
Hold on tight, Doctor.
10% power, aim—
Bingo.
Clear damage to right arm plating detected.
No reduction in speed. Showed no signs of slowing when hit, either.
Who's in that suit? Do they not feel pain?
Damage to left arm.
Damage to lower right leg.
Doctor, it's not reacting to the damage at all.
If we want to stop it, I'll need to paralyze its power core in one shot—
Come on, this makes no sense.
Even the most well-trained soldier wouldn't have this much tolerance.
You're right.
By mere fact of living, no individual can wholly suppress their most basic nervous responses.
A hand smashes through the power armor's core. Countless mechanical fragments burst out like fireworks, just as the blood of a fellow lifeform would.
With no time to waste, that hand pulls out.

The mecha-fragment extracted by her sheer force turns into dust, together with the pure white plating covering her five digits. It all drifts down to the floor, where it forms a paper-thin calcium crystal layer.
The power armor lies on the ground, the heat of its energy snuffed, a clean hole left through its torso.
You—pierced that power armor's core barehanded?
That's a might of mights. And definitely a lot quieter than my defensive cannon.
Doctor, looks like the Bureau of, uh... Originium whatever won't be taking us in for attempted bombing today.

opts: I do NOT want new criminal records for Rhodes Island.
I do NOT miss Columbian jail even a little bit.
Thanks for that, Saria.

lines.x: No need, Doctor, Mechanist.
I'm the one getting Rhodes Island involved in Rhine's internal affairs—
I have a duty to ensure you don't come to any harm.
Ferdinand was right on the money.
Rhodes Island's people have already found Saria.
Should we maintain pursuit, ma'am?
If I stuffed you inside that power armor just now, do you think you'd survive?
Relax.
Just a hypothetical.
Ma'am, no matter how powerful Saria is, there's only three of them.
We've got them way outnumbered, and outgunned with our secret weapon.
Underestimate the enemy at your own peril.
Haven't you been around Rhine Lab enough to learn? The punier a scientist looks, the harder she might just pulverize you.
Y-You mean that mechanist, or the professor-looking one with the hidden face? They can't be stronger than Rhine's Defense Director, right?
Rhodes Island... they've got talent for days.
Let's go. I'm in love with that hypothetical just now, and I've got a few new ideas I want to try out.
Good evening, Colonel.
Clooney...
Give me a rundown of what's going on at the site.
Just minor boat-rocking... You know how hard it is for Pioneers to be satisfied, no matter how generous you get with their terms.
Initiative is their strong point, and it forms the basis of Columbia's endless expansion, but sometimes it carries with it a little... hassle.
So it's hassle. Unhassle it, then.
I want to see the results you promised, not Rhine Lab in the gossip rags.
Remember, you aren't the only tech company in Columbia.
Understood.
Rest assured, Colonel.
Rhine Lab's products will not let you down.
Sir, got a whole crew wanting out of this now.
They say your plan's hopeless, that nobody's gonna listen to us. We can try everything we want, we'll never get our voices out.
They're also asking who cares what Rhine Lab's up to.
Like, we just need to get this work done sooner, get our money, move onto the next place and keep going.
As for the guys who... went missing, well, their luck just ran out. The Pioneer Teams out in the deep wastes see a lot more dead every year than we do, anyway.
I get it.
Tell them to just hold on a little longer. I'll bring our people back.
So what's your plan?
I'm gonna head out for a bit.
Out? Isn't... isn't that too dangerous?
If the cops sent sharpshooters, they might just be waiting for you to come out.
Sounds like a good deal to me.
Wh-What? You're keeping your head together, right?
A police sharpshooter, right? Might just be an old friend of mine. Crack shot with a crossbow. If this is how it's gotta be, it's not gonna hurt too bad.
Sonny...
Dr. Urbica, Doc Moore—
I need you to come out for a walk.
You can drop the pretense. Do we have a choice here?
Afraid not. Keep your chin up.
Once the folks outside get to see the score, maybe they'll change their minds, open up to taking our demands seriously.
Officer, we've got movement!
Drones, prepare to fire—
No. Hold your fire.
Officer...?
We can't hurt the hostages.
You, uh... Dr. Silence, over here.
Is there a development, ma'am?
Take a look through the binoculars. Are those your two colleagues behind S... the Pioneer?
Sir—th—the comm lit up!
Give it.
Knew it'd be you.
I could say the same thing.
You, uh... you made rank, right? Didn't get the chance to congratulate you.
Who knew you'd stay at that precinct so long... You keep getting all this hassle from the frontier.
And I never thought you'd end up so... depraved.
You still remember any of the classes you took? Your law courses?
You still remember... the shit you said to me, the day I joined the force?
'That's great! We'll be partners again. It'll be just like all those games we played as kids. We'll protect Columbia's peace, bring the bad guys to justice—'
Hah... how's that irony? You're bad guy number one right here and now.
Mary...
What's all that crap your boys are holding? Homemade weapons?
Drop those things before you embarrass yourselves any more.
You degraded yourself this far, but you still have an option. One final option.
Let those two innocent ladies next to you go.
No. That's not in the cards for now.
Dammit. You're inching over the cliff here.
Sure, the government lets a criminal join the Pioneers if he doesn't want to do hard time.
But if... you break the law again out on the frontier? What charges does that buy you?
Answer me, counsel!
Don't call me that, Mary, I'm begging you...
You're trying to guilt-trip me.
Yeah, I feel guilty now... guilty I let you go when you broke into my house. I should've hauled your ass straight to the station.
That was a long time ago. I mean, I'd only just...
...I'm sorry. I've always been sorry about that.
But this time, I have a reason for doing all this. You have to hear me out, please.
Sir!
What?
The doc, the doc, she...
Doc Moore?
Configuration... error... entering... power save mode...
Oh, shoot. Joyce...
What's happening to her, Doctor?
You can tell. She's having an attack.
What?! I—Is it Oripathy?
C'mon, I got her! There's drugs for her, right? I... I can't do injections...
Ngh...
C'mon, she looks like hell!
I... I got this first-aid treatment band Rhine gave us. This does something for Oripathy flare-ups, right?
I'll put it on her...
You're willing to give your own emergency care device to Joyce?
If you hand her that band, your odds of survival go down significantly on the off-chance you get a sudden attack.
The doc's been good to us all this time!
I don't want anything to happen to her...
You say as the ones who kidnapped me and Joyce.
We never wanted to do you dirty.
I'm sorry... We're just a bunch of selfish nobodies.
Are the comms still going?
Ma'am, would you mind giving me permission to talk to the Pioneers?
The Liberi experiencing an attack is my colleague, and my good friend.
And more important still, I am her attending physician.
I want to gather her situation from the Pioneers. Judging by their reactions, they clearly aren't unconcerned for her.
Go nuts.
But you don't have much time.
I just got orders from on high. If the Pioneers refuse to cooperate for another ten minutes, we'll cut all comms to the test site... along with all power to their living quarters.
Y—You'll cut the electricity?
Day-night temperature swings are massive in the barrens... They'll be freezing by midnight!
And there's several late-stage Oripathy patients among the Pioneers...
They need medical facilities to live!
I know.
That's what makes this a warning.
They need to know how serious Rhine and the police are here.
All their protests and demands have to be founded on a legal basis... Once they take the criminal route, nobody's going to hear them out.
But... aren't you friends with that Pioneer leader?
Friends?
The moment he abandoned himself, there was nothing left to say.
How is she?
Real-time blood-crystal density's dropping. Eye movement indices are still highly irregular.
Eye movement indices?
Due to her Oripathy's focus lying in the brain.
Any fluctuation in her condition will impact her nervous system, cause her to fall into deep sleep, and go so far as to trigger epileptic attacks.
Huh? Who's there?
The comms are still open.
Hello, sir.
I'm Joyce Moore's attending physician. My name is Silence.
Doc Silence.
Firstly, I'd like to offer you my thanks.
Joyce's condition has only stabilized so quickly with your aid, and that of the other Pioneers.
You're gonna thank us kidnappers, huh?
In return, and please trust me when I say this, we really, truly wish to assist you.
We still have five minutes before your comms, and power to your living quarters, are simultaneously cut—
Sir—w—we've got a blackout!
It seems they've acted ahead of schedule.
Don't sweat it. I gotta appreciate you being willing to warn us.
I gathered from ma'am Mary that you'd like a dialogue with Rhine Lab's upper management.
Can I ask why that is?
I wanna beg them to stop the experiments they're doing here.
To what?
You're a good doc, same as Doc Moore here. But something tells me stopping a project this big's out of your hands.
Can you tell the Rhine Lab boss that this experiment is seriously, wicked dangerous?
Sir, do you have any proof?
Even if I did, would you believe me...? A lone Pioneer at the end of his rope?
Officer, we're all set. We can cut the site's comms at any time.
Wait for Doc Silence to finish.
But the Director's demanding immediate...
Sorry, what police rank does your director hold? Remind me.
Tell fricking Ferdinand that saving people is my job. I'm not taking him on a ride-along here!
The Director's been in contact with the commissioner, Officer. They drew up this plan together.
Director... Ferdinand?
I never imagined Defense would fall under Energy's purview.
Er...
If Ferdinand feels that Structural is his to rule too, then I would not mind resigning from Rhine Lab, effective immediately.
Joyce... Joyce!
Is she... asleep?
She's lost consciousness.
If we don't get her treatment in time, she... she'll experience brain death before long.
Sir, I am preparing to enter the site now.
I understand your concerns, so I would like to reassure you, I will not be carrying any weapons.
Don't do anything stupid, Doctor!
I know full well what I am doing.
Sir, I will not allow any police officers... nor Defense personnel to follow me.
Researcher Silence, I will report your conduct to the Director... Director Parvis.
I am entering Test Site 359 under my status as a Rhodes Island Operator—
Me too...
—And my electrical engineering colleague will accompany me.
We will provide medical services, free of charge, to the Oripathy patients on-site.
Not just for my friend, but for all of you.
I do not care what choices Rhine Lab makes in the wake of this.
I only know this—treating the Infected is constant to the Rhodes Island code of conduct.

lines.st: ...Sir, your insurance premiums for the month.
That's... a lot.
I mean, I just paid a few days ago...
Then we can only cover your prescription drug costs for the rest of the month, and the policy means you'll need to get a physical at a designated health care facility before the first of the month.
Th... They still haven't given me my loan...
I've been trying, damn it. If they'd just take my parents' deli as collateral, we'd be fine...
Sir, the payment is due tomorrow.

If you can't pay your premiums on time, then you definitely can't afford follow-up treatment. Considering your health and the social issues around it, your only option would be to move into an Infected treatment zone next month.
Infected... treatment zone?
Yes.
Don't worry. You'll enjoy even deeper discounts on health care there.
No, I don't wanna...
Going in there...

lines.x: That's a dead end. My life would be over.
Sir...? Mr. Sonny?
Are you feeling alright? I heard how hard you were breathing.
It's okay, I'm not going to hurt you.
I came here with Dr. Silence, and your men checked us over. We're not carrying any dangerous weapons.
Sorry. I just, uh, force of habit.
No need to apologize. I understand.
It's always hard when the darkness creeps in.
Could you come a little closer? Body heat makes things much warmer. You won't go numb this way, even if you've got to sleep.
Where's the others?
Dr. Silence's medical drone installation has an emergency power source.
She's inside the building right now giving Ptilopsis a health checkup, and Miss Elena's with them too.
Sam's...
He's followed your plan. He got all the Pioneers in poorer health together, in there.
On it.
Lemme ask you a favor. Don't tell Sam, or anyone, how I just, uh...
I understand.
You're their leader. If they thought your strength wasn't holding up at such a key moment, you could lose control of the Pioneer Team.
I'm not trying to say your team isn't united enough, but... the darkness usually has a way of pushing on variables.
You've got a mile of experience.
I'm a Bolívarian. Blackouts were pretty common back in the city I'm from.
Bolívar... there's a war there?
We never had an easy guess for where the fires of war would stall out, back home.
Must've been hard for you. Getting yourself out of there, I mean.
It was all thanks to my colleagues.
The way I see it, you and your Pioneer Team are even more amazing.
Me... amazing? Hahah...
Wh-Why are you laughing?
So those stupid company ads made it all the way down to Bolívar?
No, I don't mean like that.
No matter how you look at it, you got here by your own two feet. And the ones who came before you, the immigrants from Victoria, Gaul, and Bolívar...
It's their footprints all together that lit up these wilds.
Hah... you got a way with words.
Eh? I'm just telling it like it is.
I'd always wanted to see what the famous Columbian frontier was like.
Well, you disappointed? Might be more desolate than your hometown here.
No, Mr. Sonny, I think I've learned a lot.
For example, the way your power lines are designed really is economical and efficient, just as Mechanist described.
Officer, the vehicle's ready.
Make sure you've got the right equipment.
I saw them with handmade crossbows.
Let it get a little darker, then send a few drones ahead to the Pioneer living quarters. Confirm the position of every hostage and bandit.
We follow behind, and look for a good opportunity to bust in.
Then... what about Researcher Silence and that Rhodes Island operator?
What are you going for here?
I'm not sure which side they're on.
Is that how you Rhine Labbers work? You've gotta pick a side first no matter what you're doing?
We just want to ensure all problems are resolved.
That's what we do in Defense, Officer.
And what I do is help people.
Listen, I don't care what you guys want to do. My advice is to make sure you don't get in the way of my job either.
So you're saying it's Rhodes Islanders who saved you?
Rhodes Island didn't just save my life in the accident, they gave me the chance to continue my education.
I'm where I am today all because Rhodes Island changed my life.
Hahah... what a nice company.
Any other time and place, I would've thought you were running a recruitment drive.
Mr. Sonny—if you do have an interest, you can absolutely send your CV to Rhodes Island.
Me, a Pioneer, and looking like a kidnapper now... handing my CV to a company as fine as you're talking about?
Pretty funny joke there.
Humble yourself all you want, but I can still tell how much potential you got as an engineer.
Not to mention your three ladies in there... They're all Infected, and they still got hired by Rhine Lab? Do you know how tough that is?
Um... yeah, they definitely are outstanding...
No, not just outstanding. They're one in a million. Top of their fields.
You got so many Infected each year, and only the tiniest number of talents ever get 'pardoned.'
But Rhodes Island...
Yeah, Mr. Greyy, you want to tell me, 'Rhodes Island never gives terms that harsh when it saves people.'

Even if I was willing to believe you, believe your special kind of faith in the company that saved your life, believe it was ready to go against its own nature, its self-interest and treat an Infected fair and square.
You still can't tell me you're not lucky to be one of the chosen ones.

And the saps left over, they get round up to sit and wait until they drown in the mud. Their only other chance is to try taking a few steps, face up to bigger danger, and go looking for the tiniest place where just for a moment, they can rest their feet.
Them and me... we're all in the same boat. No soul to save us. Can't even save ourselves.
Olivia, do you really think this is going to help?
Joyce's brain activity indicators have stabilized somewhat.
I've administered some intravenous nervous depressants. That used to be a routine treatment method. The effect isn't especially ideal in this case, but...
Could her attack have had some other trigger?
Isn't she still in serious danger either way?
Yes. As long as we can't treat the cause, she needs a better care environment.
That's great... but I'm not even asking about Joyce's condition.
You let Greyy and the Pioneer leader go on patrol together.
That wasn't my decision. It was Greyy's own idea.
You're the captain.
I haven't spent as much time on the Rhodes landship as you two have, but I know how the company works, Olivia. I'm not trying to criticize you...
But if Greyy runs into trouble, or we're held up treating Joyce, you won't forgive yourself.
Do you believe Sonny would more easily trust Greyy, or us?
Is there a difference?
I recall you were unfortunate enough to catch Oripathy in a testing accident at Rhine headquarters.
Yes.
Did it cross your mind at the time... that you might never be able to do research again?
Of course it did.
But the boss—I mean, Ferdinand sent a new contract right out, extending the three years to ten, and giving me a thirty percent raise.
You were Employee of the Year.
Yeah... not much of a consolation prize, right?
I mean, all the time I was getting treated at Rhodes Island, I ended up writing the director three entire conference papers.
I understand your perspective; I'm more or less the same.
I'm far less purely optimistic than you. I've worried about the future, and following from that, I was thankful to Parvis and Rhine Lab, for a time.
But I... I've never doubted my own abilities, nor what they could do for Rhine Lab in return.
For us, Oripathy is a challenge to overcome; it's not going to gnaw our entire lives away.
And that's why... Greyy can do what we can't.
Mr. Sonny, I'd like to point out, there's a kind of contradiction in what you're saying.
For real?
You say that the Pioneers risk it all in search of the next place to rest their feet...
So how do you know we're not about to come across a way out?
Sir, the building—the power's back!
What the hell?!
Hm...
Wait, you son of a gun—that's what you were doing the whole time you were talking to me?
Mr. Sonny...
If I hadn't run out into the darkness back then, I wouldn't ever have met the Rhodes Islanders who came before me, carrying the light.
Trust me. Sometimes, taking one more step really does mean something.
Even if... I'm already standing on the brink?
—Even if you've already jumped off.
We've seen you reaching out your hand. You're crying out to us for help.
So we'll do everything we can to catch you.
No matter what you've been through, it's far from time to give up yet.
We've got power!
Did Greyy... do it?
Hngh...
That's a relief. Joyce is showing a reaction to the light.
Trust you to be so focused, even now.
Ladies.
Why are you back alone? Where's Greyy?
This is the only place with power right now. He's making for the other quarters.
You don't trust me, do you?
Stay back!
Elena?
Olivia, you said he wouldn't trust us, but...
I have even less grounds to trust him.
He tricked me and Joyce into coming here, and I've kept quiet on this the whole time... I saw more of them surrounding the labs.
All of those Pioneers are armed!
It's not what you think...
What do I think, huh? You said you didn't want to do anyone dirty! You mean you forgot how Dorothy's been treating you all?!
You're going to hurt her for your own selfish ends!
Is that...? Did you pull an alarm?
I've been planning to for a long time. I'm sure the police and my Defense colleagues are already on the way. They won't need any drones to pin our position now.
Much thanks to Greyy for this.
So that's why he got the power back on...
Don't project your own gloomy mind on him.
Greyy really does want to help you, just like Dorothy and Joyce do.
Out of respect for them, I'll consider letting you off the hook.
Sir, whatever plans you had before, give them up before it's too late.
Are the drones out?
Not yet. Our people are waiting for your order.
Then tell me... what the hell is that thing shining above the site?!
So close. It'll all be good soon.
That's some... goddamned beautiful light.
Is it? Thank you...
Hahah... Got me feeling thawed out some, even, working with you here.
Just that bit left in front now, right? We'll show those megacorpers! Cut whatever you want to us, we won't take it sitting down, not while we've still got our own two hands to get things done!
Wait, something's wrong...
Hold on a second!
What is it?
D—Don't go any further! That light isn't my Arts!
Doc Silence.
Hm...
I'd like to ask just one more time. Are you really willing to trust a Pioneer?
I'm a doctor, and a researcher.
I'm like my colleagues—
In that I trust the truth.
What's the deal outside?
What are they all running for? Did you—
No, not on my orders.
Get away from me, you bastard—!
Shit!
The police are probably here to rescue us.
No, stay in here!
What the hell is wrong with you now—
My guess is, that thing's come again...
What thing?
Well, the... truth, I wanted to tell you about.
Elena, keep away from the door!
Sir—s—sir...
Got...ta watch... out...
Has he been hurt? Or is he... having an attack?
Stay away from him.
Your teammate is clearly in need of assistance!
Watch what's behind him—
An irregularly shaped object hangs in the air.
Its surface is exceptionally glossy, issuing gentle silver rays all about, and it hovers faintly up and down at this moment, as if following some sort of breathing pattern.
After rising up behind the Pioneer, about three seconds pass where it hangs in the air.
Then immediately, it slowly rotates its own body, as if curiously assessing every human at the scene.
This... It's...
Run!!!
I have to admit, I wasn't expecting that.

opts: Neither was I.
Up until I saw Saria do it...

lines.x: Rest assured. I have no intention of going back to prison either.
Then that makes cleaning up the scene of the crime here a lot easier.
After all, I'm a mechanist. Won't take much time to pull a machine apart.
But I'm wondering... How did you know it was empty inside?
I've sparred with this power suit a few times before.
You've been hunted by them too?
In a sense.

opts: They're not out for Saria.
Could be the other way around, honestly...

lines.x: If they're showing up here, that goes to show someone really doesn't want us tracking Muelsyse's whereabouts.
Who's that someone going to be?
There are many possibilities.
I don't contact Muelsyse much. I only know she was participating in some Rhine Lab-headed secret experiment before she disappeared.
Considering I left Rhine long ago, her divulging as much to me already violated her NDA.
Does Rhine Defense dispatch cutting-edge weapons to eliminate info-leaking employees?
Got it. That's non-disclosure too.
Rhodes Island sure is a friendly enterprise, isn't it, Dr. Doctor?

opts: Rhodes Island isn't just an enterprise.
How else did we get either of you?

lines.x: Muelsyse isn't an ordinary Rhine Lab employee.
If they can drive her to the edge, their professionalism has to be on par with mine.
This model of power armor made a showing in certain mercenary groups about half a year ago.
Neither its defensive material nor its equipped weaponry are outstanding. All in all, it's comparable to military-manufactured power armor from five years ago.
Save for one very notable point—it doesn't demand anything from its user.
Anything... how low do the requirements go?
Any being whose physiological parameters can maintain a standard exercise state satisfies the requirements.
I can, you can, and the Doctor can.

opts: Yeah, that's low.
I'm kind of dying to try.

lines.x: You won't like the feeling of it, Doctor, I can guarantee you.
Only crack troops used to be able to wear power armor.
Picture controlling that giant contraption for extended periods of fighting, and not drowning in your own vomit in the process—it genuinely wasn't something anyone could do without training.
Ask Dur-nar, she'll tell you. She says she wore it one time ever, and it made her want to quit the military right then and there.
I imagine these intervening years have been enough for them to iterate the technology a couple dozen times over, and there's no shortage of vets among the mercenaries...
But for anyone to wear it?
That's an invention that might change how whole wars are fought.
Similar power armors are only out there in extremely low numbers, which makes it obvious this tech is only in its prototype phase.
I'm on the trail of its source right now. Getting leads isn't a cakewalk, as with so many other experimental weapons.

opts: Are Columbia's mercenaries that rich?
This power armor's extremely advanced tech, right?

lines.x: The mercs may not be clients.
In the eyes of the people who order this stuff, mercenaries and these latest power suits are interchangeable—
—It's only experimental.
The edge projected from nomadic cities is often the boundary where the rules of civilization no longer apply.
The mercenaries active in the barrens, same as the Pioneers, are the most convenient test subjects.
Even if an experiment fails, and they're disposed of, hardly anyone is going to pursue their whereabouts.
And soon enough, the next batch of test subjects gets funneled in.

opts: Don't any of them fight it?

lines.x: No point in fighting it.
The mercenaries are fully aware the new weapons in their hands could explode, but they could also be stronger than whatever their opponents are wielding.
Legally, these mutually consenting deals are on the verge of compliance.
Mr. Mechanist, have you discovered anything new?
Something feels off...
Something's felt off from the very beginning.
Nobody in the power armor—how did I not tell that at a glance?
The mercs could be remote-controlling it from a distance, or maybe they set it up with automated programming—but no matter what, there'd be distinct patterns in its behavior.

opts: Just like Closure's drones.
Just like Lancet-2 and Castle-3.

lines.x: Obviously, they're not that flexible.
Even the highest grade of automation out of the newest crop of Rhodes Island's R&D weaponry wouldn't escape my eyes—you want me to believe that bunch of dumb army-funded mechanists could pull it off?
What are you doing?
Severing the power armor's 'central nerves.'
There's something dripping out of these tubes... a silver liquid.
What is this substance?
Maybe it's the answer to your question.
Did we... get away?
*pant*... I... I haven't run this fast in a long time...
What exactly was...
It followed us?!
Get down!
This isn't the same one as before...
There're at least ten—even more, actually. They've surrounded the living quarters.
They're... multiplying.
Multiplying? Have you seen those things before? Explain!
Dr. Urbica, shouldn't you be the one explaining?
What the ever-loving hell do you mean...?
Move—!
—This thing's too fast!
I don't want to imagine what happens if it touches you—do you think we should head back inside?
Better we don't.
Why?
That's... the building we were in...
Under an invisible, massively powerful attack, the Pioneers' hut violently sways, the flimsy windows and doors shattering in quick succession.
At practically the same time, the peculiar objects that were hanging in the air before fly straight at the room.
Silver liquid oozes through the cracks in every frame at lightning speed, just as a minute creature would smell blood and dig fearlessly into its prey's wounds.
But in the next instant, something that defies all comprehension, including the scientists', decides to take place.
Did that building just... disappear?
No. It didn't disappear—it was disintegrated.
What?
Do you see those... things floating in the air?
You're trying to tell me that—used to be part of the building?
I fear that may be the entire building.
Just as her voice falls silent, the fragments frozen in midair come falling down one after another.
Cacophony, dust, detritus everywhere, like the restarting of a clock stopped by force—and everything that existed by all rights returns.
Did... Did I get hit by some kind of mind-control Arts?
I mean, if not, then everything I just saw... somehow violated every law of physics I know.
It's the work of those silver liquids.
They... dispersed into innumerable minute individuals, dismantled and lifted away what was a section of that room...
And then reassembled it.
Like there were dozens of invisible... hands?
Sounds like you ladies are talking about ghosts.
Wait, Ptilopsis—
Shit, Joyce was just in that room!
Don't tell me she got...
*cough*... *cough* *cough*...
Myers! You made it out too?
S—Sir... I got... Doc Moore... outta there...
Joyce!
Th—Thank God... she's okay...
His wounds aren't severe, but his endurance has been overtaxed. He urgently needs rest.
We can't hang around this place. None of the Pioneer quarters are safe anymore.
Did you see that?
I don't know what you're referring to.
Knock it off, I know you're not that dumb. Did you not feel that tremor just now?
Were those floaty things some new model of drone or something? You Rhiners developed those yourself? Dammit!
I can only tell you that we did not take any action against the site, on your orders.
Who was your boss, again? Ferdinand?
Mr. Ferdinand Clooney is the Director of Rhine Lab's Energy Section.
I don't care how your company's structured.
Give me your comm. I want to talk to him.
Director Clooney is extremely busy...
Then I'm gonna need you to pass along a message—
Ask if he knows what a bastard he is!
Director, you have a message, sent from observation at the site.
Well, well...
Also, your son appears to need you for something. Should I put him through?
Same play. Tell him I'm busy.
Right, send the contents of Elena's most recent report to my terminal.
On it, Director.
How many times have I told you? Don't call me Director.
Ah! Sorry, Mr. Clooney.
Speaking of Elena...
I gather you get along well with her.
Brainstorm for me. What kind of present would she like?
...Olivia.
I'm sorry, treating the wounded Pioneers has me occupied...
Are you hurt?
I'm... fine.
I don't have field experience like you do, but I can take care of myself, at least.
Even then... shouldn't we be evacuating ASAP?
The whole site's in chaos... The Pioneers are busy with themselves. We've got a good opportunity to escape.
I'm positive the director got my message. He'll send people to fetch us.
Once we meet back up with our colleagues at Defense, we'll be in a perfect position to wipe out the threat to the site, and save Dorothy.
Maybe you're right.
Maybe?
But I can't do it. It's not in my power to leave these people for dead.
Just now, they couldn't do it... leave Joyce for dead. You saw.
Doc Silence, we still got a few casualties here!
Drone—!
We're not gonna last much longer if they keep this up.
I've tried every way to attack it, no dice. Those freaks don't hurt, and they're impossible to break.
We don't even... got nowhere to hide.
Sir, why are you telling them all this?
They could be getting off on it!
These things above our heads are all mystery weapons sent out by the fuzz and the megacorps! They want us trapped in this test site forever—
Sam, lay off that for now.
*cough* *cough*...
Everyone, I... I've got an idea...
Greyy... do you have some kind of proposal?
*huff*... *huff*... *cough* *cough* *cough*...
Take your time. You were running too fast. Be careful not to bruise yourself.
I've noticed—those objects seem to chase after light, and human voices.
Watch...
They're following your Arts' light ball, away into the air.
*pant*... It's only temporary.
My Arts aren't able to trick them for long.
You've done all you can. And very well, at that.
Phototaxis... You've brought up an interesting line of thought.
Greyy, can you assist in evacuating the nearby Pioneers? They trust you.
Move them somewhere dark, and tell them they need to keep quiet.
Sure, I know what to do.
I've done work like this plenty of times with the senior operators...
Dr. Silence, you and Miss Elena take care too.
What about us, then?
Make some signs of movement. The larger, the better. It has to cover the sounds of the others' footsteps at minimum.
On it.
Sam, call five or six guys over if you can, take whatever gear makes the most noise.
Are you seriously going along with them?
You got a better idea?
Whatever you say, boss.
Draw every silver body to our location.
So we act as bait—and then what?
For now, stall as long as we can.
And then come to the laboratories.
That voice...
Dorothy?!
Yes, it's me. Dorothy.
I did a quick rig on the laboratory PAs—I hope I didn't scare you.
Are you alright?
I'm completely safe. You don't need to worry about me.
The laboratory buildings are sturdier, and they will help you resist the threat.
Everyone—be you Pioneer Team friends, or Rhine coworkers, the laboratories' doors are open to you.

lines.st: What is the nature of Originium Arts?
The Spire Casters of Leithanien and the academicians of Columbia each developed their own separate theories.
Of course, both are simply packaging for the benefit of their respective adherents. Regardless of any differences in terminology, the two theories describe the same relationships.
Originium Arts involve the caster exerting influence on external matter through a medium, i.e., an Arts Unit.
The Arts Unit receives and encodes the caster's neural activity, releasing it as energy that interacts with matter or its component particles, resulting in the desired effect.
Modern Arts Units are the product of advances in both theory and industry, with complex energy conversion circuits at their core. Clearly, not all Originium products are suited for this purpose.
However, we cannot ignore how the Infected are able to use Arts without an Arts Unit, using their infected organs to similar effect.
We've all heard the terrifying rumors of Leithanian Spire Casters using one or more Infected as a medium for casting, in effect turning them into massive energy conversion circuits.
Of course, such blatant ethical violations would never be allowed in Columbia.
What about safe, artificial implants that could serve the same purpose, though?

Just as those who've lost their legs in accidents can walk again with prosthetics, or those born blind can see with cornea transplants, those without the ability to use Originium Arts may, one day, harness the same power.
I'm seeing some shocked faces in the audience. Let me emphasize that this is all hypothetical for now.
But I was invited here today by my alma mater to talk about the research that our institute is currently conducting.
And ever since I was student, I've wanted to do more for Columbia using Originium Arts, for everyone in this rapidly evolving society.
Leithanien distributes social resources according to Originium Arts Assimilation.
Here in Columbia, however, we believe that all men are created equal.
Address to the Trimounts Institute of Technology by Loken Williams, founder of Loken Watertank Laboratory

lines.x: Composition analysis failed.
Liquid does not match any known compound.
Look.
White crystals form at her fingertips, spreading to cover her entire hand and forearm.
It is a scene that you just saw, and which is as familiar to you from combat reports as from your own experience.

opts: Calcification?
Danger?!

lines.x: Saria does not answer.
Suddenly, she raises her hand, and you find her fist in front of your nose faster than you can blink.
You hear the sound of mineral impacting metal, and know that Mechanist blocked the attack for you.
Before you can catch your breath, you feel something cold touch your neck.
Saria's other hand never moved.
But there is a calcified blade at your throat, nonetheless.
You instinctively hold your breath.
Explain yourself, Saria.
This is the quickest way to explain it.
My Originium Arts reconstruct matter containing calcium, at a molecular level. This is what you call calcification.
I can control the enamel formed this way using my mind, without the need to move.
No radio signals or programs.
They are like an extension of my limbs.
Do you mean the power armor—
A power armor is nothing more than a power armor.
This silvery liquid is the key.
It's the neurotransmitter of the power armor, linking the brainwaves of the operator to the machine.
Like an Arts Unit?
More than that, I should say.

opts: An intuitive explanation.
I need to catch my breath...

lines.x: Sorry if I've caused any discomfort.
I would not have gone for the Doctor's hand, were it not for that gesture to me.
Good thing we know each other so well.
There's someone I want to see.
As far as I know, he's the first to actually use this prototype power armor.
I would like you to come, with Mechanist.
You saw them go into a bar near the university?
Yes, ma'am. All three of them.
I can guess who it is Saria's looking for.
We just saw him last week.
Didn't he agree not to meet anyone from Rhine Lab?
Is Saria still Rhine?
I don't think she knows the answer herself.
Should we stop them?
No.
I'm curious how much Saria knows.
It's about time we had a talk.
And one more thing... Loken Williams is a graduate of the Trimounts Institute of Technology.
Loken Williams...? Who is that?
I guess time really does fly.
One accident, and everyone forgets the brightest star of academia.
Saria and the Control of Rhine Lab went to the same school.
Social gatherings in Columbia's tech sector are basically all TIT reunions.
I wish I could've spent some time at that school myself.
Let's go. Maybe it's not too late to join the fun.
The last fire bomb!
Throw it! Far as you can!
Here goes!
With me! Get inside!
We got everybody?
Whew... yes, sir. All present and accounted for.
What about the monsters?
Not yet. The flask's still burning, looks like it's got their attention.
Thank you, everyone.
We have all of the injured.
What's next, Doc Silence?
Sir, the door—
It won't open! What the hell is going on?!
You lock that door, Doc?
I've never been to this test site before.
Dr. Urbica?
The lab doors are automatic.
But it's locked!
Doctor...
What are you complaining about? Aren't you afraid of those silver constructs outside?
How're we supposed to know if you're trying to keep them out, or keep us in?
Maybe you planned all this!
Are you joking?
Hah! You're saying I somehow made you kidnap me?
Watch it! You're our prisoner, don't forget!
Sam!
You're the one who said these whitecoats are fooling us!
No, be quiet for a sec—
You hear it, Doc Silence? A rustle, like something slithering on the ground...
Do not stop, and do not look back.
Push open the third door on your right.
It opened!
Do not be afraid. I will protect you.
As always.
Test results for the latest batch of Transmitter?
Yes, sir.
In a laboratory environment, of course...
A one-point-seven percent increase in efficiency.
A massive breakthrough in one month.
Excellent work. Do you need more time? Ten days? Twenty?
S-Sorry! Whoa...
Come now, don't frighten the youths like that.
No wonder the staff so fear the accolades of the Director of Energy.
Come to boast about your people skills, Parvis?
Just passing through.
Back to your lab now, poor little fellow, I'll keep your director company for a bit.
Er, alright, ok!
Thank you...
Ahah! So quick on his feet. Such is youth, eh? Gone in a flash.
I don't see you outside your lab often, except for directors' meetings.
What brings you here, exactly?
Where do I even start? The brain doesn't spin so fast at my age.
You were born with white hair, Caprinae.
Haha.
Oh, now I remember. Do you know where Muelsyse is?
We made a bet. She wagered ten boxes of black bean tea from hydroponics in Ecological.
A bet?
Two days ago, she told me that you were planning to rid Ferdinand Clooney's name of the 'Director' title.
Sonny...?
Why did you stop?
We can't do this.
Do what?
We can't follow that voice.
The voice... you mean Director Franks?
Sonny, Director Franks knows this lab better than anyone, better than Elena or myself. She knows where we'll be safe.
That's why I don't trust her!
Don't you see? She's pulling our strings, guiding every step, drilling into our minds...
She's walking us right into her cage, and we're going along with it!
Please calm down, Sonny. Getting excited could exacerbate your condition.
Lemme go! Get away from me!
Oof...
Olivia! Are you okay?
I'm fine.
That's enough, Pioneer.
I would have left you outside, if Olivia didn't insist we help you.
Ha, like we wanted to be here with you in the first place.
I don't understand. At all.
Dorothy was so kind to you.
How's your son, Dirk?
Not so good, Director Franks. The doctor says he needs an operation.
But...
Is it the cost?
Nothing gets past you. I joined the Pioneer Team to try to help my boy. This is my first project.
We don't have much money. I shouldn't've spent any on Oripathy medicine. If only I knew my son had heart trouble.
There's no hope for me, but him... he's still so little...
Don't worry, Dirk. I know a way.
Here's the best heart surgeon I know. Take your son and find her.
The best surgeon? In Trimounts?
Must be awful pricey...
She's an old classmate of mine. Tell her Dorothy Franks sent you, and she'll give you a discount.
R-Really?
Don't tell anybody. It'd be bad for her reputation if word got out that she gives discounts.
Won't tell a soul! Cross my heart!
I... I gotta go! Thank your friend for me!
Why are you standing there? The coffee's getting cold.
So much the better.
It'll hurt less when I spill it on you.
Are you worried about me, or the coffee?
You can smile and plead all you want, but I'm not lending you lunch money.
Looks like it's plain rice for the rest of the month then.
A director of Rhine Lab, begging for food from a low-level researcher... can you imagine the scandal?
Originium Art Section is the poorest of the five, after all.
I'm not Ferdinand, I don't have sponsors lined up waiting to dress me in gold from head to tail.
At least the boss has good taste.
Back on topic, it's not like I don't know the classmate you're talking about. She probably couldn't wait to double her fee when she heard your name.
You're willing to pay anything for your Pioneer friends. No wonder people take advantage of you.
Hahah...
Laugh all you want. Not like I can do anything about it.
I'm going back to my lab.
Maybe finishing this experiment will help stuff your wallet a little.
She did so much for you, and pushed me into keeping it secret.
How could you do this to her?
Dr. Urbica, you're Dorothy Franks's friend.
You see only what she wants you to see.
You don't know what terrible things she's hiding under the surface.
Director Franks! My boy's surgery went great. The doctor says he'll be out of the hospital by next week!
Fantastic. Would you like to go home and see him?
G-Go home? Back to the city?
B-But I'm Infected. I can't enter a nomadic city on my own...
Unless you have a new contract that pays for the insurance.
You're looking at a chance, Dirk. One that can change your destiny.
Sign this consent form, and you'll be able to go home.
Of course I...
No rush. You have twenty-four hours to think about it.
This isn't a trick, nothing in the fine print.
I've circled the important points. Make sure you understand the risks.
Come find me if you have any questions.
Dirk!
S-Sir? What're you doing here? Did you sign up for...
Give me the consent form.
W-Why? No!
Don't you get it? She's stuffing you into a cage, to experiment on!
S-So what?
Director Franks says she's giving me a chance.
When was the last time we heard the word 'chance'?
I don't need twenty-four hours.
I won't wait another minute for a chance to see my boy!
That's how she got Dirk.
Not just Dirk. Alan, Gale, Sophia...
She knew everyone's weaknesses. No one could say no to her.
That's Dorothy Franks!
Those silver creatures were her...
The silver constructs?
They're... *cough* *cough*... you know nothing!
How much do the two of you know about our pursuers?
One of you calls them creatures, the other uses the word constructs.
I'm certain I heard correctly. My people are renowned among Liberi for our sense of hearing.
I'm just guessing.
Same here.
At least you agree on something.
Never mind that. No matter what you think of Dorothy, at this moment, she's helping us escape.
Let's follow her instructions for now.
Maybe our pursuers on the other end of the corridor will help you collect your thoughts.
This smell is familiar...
Too bad there's still work to be done. I miss the sensation of alcohol rushing through my body.

opts: I've heard your special drink is machine oil.
I've heard you stand on a chair and recite poems when you get drunk.

lines.x: You're listening to Blaze, huh?
She's always complaining about all the rumors there are about her, and complaining that I take things too rough in her weapon test sessions.
'Machine oil' is the name of my favorite cocktail.
Regular patrons of Just One More Pint know it well.
If you want to dig deep into those kinds of rumors, Doctor, take Blaze up on it next time she invites you to drink.
Logos and the others will—
Let's just say that what you see will make you forget any rumors about me.
Have you found your man, Saria?
He spends twenty hours a day here.
Two Trimounts Sunshines, please.
You don't look like someone who drinks during work hours.
I'm not.
I've never been one to let alcohol interfere with my brain, regardless of the hour.
Live a little, Saria.
Both of these are yours.
And you'd better pull yourself together, because I'm not buying a third.
What? Have you gone homeless too, after leaving Rhine?
How many drinks I'm willing to buy for you depends on how much your information is worth to me.
Well then, spit it out. What do you want to know?
See the liquid in this test tube?
Hm? Hmm...
It's not for drinking.
Well then, why are you asking me?
Show me your right hand.
What are you doing?
You're right-handed, but you're holding the glass with your left.
Does your right arm still hurt from the subdermal Arts Unit implant?
The what?
Saria...
Who's the helmet, and the hood?
Rhine Engineering? Volvort Kochinski? Maybe... one of those places whose name we dare not speak?
Did they change their minds? Decide to shut me up forever?
I promise you, I have nothing to do with any of the organizations you mentioned. Not now, not ever.
Promise? Words like that ain't even worth the booze in front of me.
Doctor, he may not be willing to talk with us here.
It's you I don't trust, especially not that getup.
You reek of the Columbian tech elite, know what I mean?
The hood can stay. I feel better about you.

opts: Why don't you take a break, Mechanist?
How about you go get yourself a drink?

lines.x: Alright, I'll be over there.
Saria, you've picked up so many tabs for me, let me give you some honest advice.
Don't dig any deeper, if you don't want to end up like me.
This test tube of yours, that power armor—you're biting off more than you can chew.
Yes, more than even you can chew.
Do you understand what it is you've just said?
Of course.
It was you who reminded me. I'm not senile yet.
This is a very serious accusation.
Is it now? An accusation?
Oh, the accusation that you have something to do with Muelsyse's disappearance? Or the one that you're trying to seize control of Rhine Lab?
You need stronger evidence than that to blackmail me.
Relax, old friend.
How long have we worked together? Ten years and then some?
Did you think I was the type to come chat with an old friend carrying a bunch of papers, voice recordings and legal drafts?
You...
Just because you did it to me doesn't mean I intend to return the favor.
Columbians like to put everything on the negotiating table. But you forget, I'm a cunning old Leithanian goat, who likes to play dumb.
What exactly do you want?
Just passing by, and to encourage an old friend.
I don't understand. I thought you were on Control's side.
You don't understand? We're researchers, after all. There's always more we don't understand than we do. That's part of the fun.
You're a lot like Kristen. Too young, and too smart.
You're always trying to fly high, not realizing how difficult it is for the others to keep up with you.
All I want is to be left in peace to do my research, and not be dragged along the ground.
It may only take a couple of years of your administration for the military and big capital to pick Rhine Lab to the bone, but it won't happen tomorrow.
I'm surprised you have such a high opinion of me.
You'll need to deal with the Director of Defense first, however, in order to get to Kristen.
I didn't think Defense had a director.
Look at you, your memory failing at such a tender age.
Kristen never accepted Saria's resignation.
I never understood how you think, Saria.
No one talks about that ever since everything went down at Loken... my old employer.
They all chose to cut ties, no matter how much or how little they knew about the experiments.
That's what smart people do.
Does that mean you're not smart?
Ha... tongue still as sharp as ever, huh?
I would run if I could. They won't let me.
You ask if my right hand hurts? Of course it does! But did you think they would let me live this long if I didn't give up my hand and become a test subject?
I was the goddamned surgeon! Even if I had no idea what they wanted me to implant!
Still capable of anger, I see. Looks like alcohol hasn't completely numbed you.
You've changed, Saria.
I could see you were angry when you first came to me, a few years ago.
Oh?
You weren't exactly wearing it on your face.
But I could see it. I felt the same way, when I learned what kind of experiments I was involved in.
There are other ways, if you want to take down Rhine Lab.
You're one of the founders. Anybody after Rhine will be queueing up to take your hand.
Taking down Rhine won't solve anything.
Kill one beast, only for its flesh and blood to feed yet more beasts. The fall of Loken Watertank is proof.
Excuses.
You've gone all over the place for Rhine. You're opposed to them on the surface, but what you're actually doing is cleaning up their messes.
When I saw you, I knew the rumors were true.
You were willing to go to jail for Rhine Lab.

opts: The Mansfield incident?
Sounds right, but something feels off...
