lines.x: Not the smartest thing to deal in rumors.
The fall of HydeBro was a headache for the Director of Energy, wasn't it?
The loss of a key supplier has thrown a wrench into his requests for a new laboratory site. He lost a few major orders, didn't he?
It was a setback to whatever experiments he had going on.
It wasn't me who sent the Hyde brothers to jail.
You played a big part.
I know the rules. With the Hyde brothers locked up, the prosecutors no doubt wanted to use them to hook bigger fins.
Why was there no mention of Rhine Lab in the news? Why does Rhine always emerge unscathed from these things?
The truth is you've been following the trail, cleaning up all Rhine's little mistakes left along the way.
That's why you came looking for me...
You wanted to know whether or not Rhine had anything to do with this reagent.
Deep down, you're still the Rhine's Director of Defense. You still think it's your job to pick up after them.
There's something at Rhine that you can't let go of. Or maybe it's someone. Some sentiment.
Ha, looks like you do have emotions after all, Saria!
At the end of the day, we're all flesh and blood.
The subject is out of control!
Restraints have failed!
Not getting any neural signals from the subject! Attempting sedative injection... negative!
The subject is activating Originium Arts!
A monster... it's a huge monster...
No, the flames are manifesting...
Get the Director on the line, now!
Fire... the fire... cough...
Ifrit, where are you...?
Ifrit!
Saria...?
She felt no heat in the fiery corridor.
The eyes of the Vouivre were harder than rock, colder than steel.
She could not read the emotions within them.
To this day, she does not know what she saw in the Vouivre's eyes.
Are you okay, Doc Silence?
You've gotta be tired.
You've been taking care of Doc Moore, and the rest of us.
I'm wicked sorry I acted like that, and almost got you hurt.
Rhodes Island isn't like Rhine Lab. You and Greyy have been so good to us, and you never asked for nothing for it.
Is that true?
I'm no saint, Sonny.
I was a Rhine Lab researcher. My experiments almost wiped out an entire city block, countless people along with it...
Huh?
There was a girl. She was close to me... almost like family.
She was the subject of the experiment.
What happened to her?
She's still alive.
But only alive. All these years, I've tried and failed to give her back the life she should have had, a normal life.
But I couldn't stop the situation from deteriorating.
Don't blame yourself. I'm sure you never meant to hurt her.
Ha... haha...
Funny, that's how I comforted myself in the past.
I didn't know. It was others who were truly to blame.
But was that really so?
As the overseer in the field, did I really not know the risks of our research?
The answer is clear.
Either I was blinded by the tantalizing promise of a breakthrough...
Or I was far less a researcher than I thought I was.
And that's harder to do than to hate someone else.
Perhaps the one I truly could not forgive was...
I never knew your story.
It's more than just a story. It's why I came here.
Aren't you trying to save your friend?
Of course, I have to save Joyce.
But I want to save you, and Elena, and Director Franks.
I want to stop all this, before we get to the point of no return.
You believe me?
Like I said, I believe in the truth, in what I see for myself.
And I recall that I've seen something like it.
The silver things following us—they look like the result of Originium Arts gone awry.
By the way, I had Ben bring you something. It should be on your desk now.
Ben?
You forgot again? You told him to have tea with me. He talked so much that he nearly drank my entire stash.
I don't remember you owing me anything.
Oh? That wasn't what you said when I'd just completed my chimeric organism experiments.
The Diαbolic Experiments.
Glad you remember.
My student used an anesthetic of her own design during the final phase.
To shocking results.
Silence was such a smart girl. I miss her.
After the project was suspended, I reviewed the data logs and found that it was no coincidence.
What data?
You're not the only one who digs through the trash.
You mean Loken Watertank? Those data were...
Let's not dwell on clerical matters, shall we?
Loken Williams was a genius. Reviewing his data made me realize that I was taking the long way round.
I was trying to increase the subject's cell activity, in order to increase sync rate with the energy contained within the embedded shards.
Who would have thought that suppressing the subject's neural responses would be the way to go?
Suppress?
I made some small changes to your formula based on this conjecture.
I hope you'll forgive the initiative.
You're a sly one, old goat.
Why the irritation? I simply offered a gift before you asked for it.
Is it really so disappointing to lose a weapon against me?
It had better be of use to my experiments.
I promise you'll like it.
Oh, and if you're successful, could you pick up the tab on the ten boxes of black bean tea that Muelsyse owes me?
I would have won that bet, if not for my formula.
Back on topic.
The reagent.
I had a sniff when you took it out.
Yes, it's the exact same smell.
Ha, it's the ghosts of the ones who died on my operating table, back to haunt me.
Thicker and more numerous...
Who?
Who, you ask?
Who salvaged the most from the ruins of Loken Watertank?
Time to go home, Saria.
Rhine Lab.
See, you knew the answer all along.
You already met the Tin Man. He helped you find me.
Who was the main sponsor of the Diαbolic Experiments? Who was Loken Watertank's investor?
Who had the means to hire multiple labs to engage in similar experiments at the same time?
How irresistible did the order have to be, to make those labs put their petty patent feuds aside and sit down to share data?
And...
Whose hands did the surviving subjects pass through before they were sent out of Columbia?

opts: Do you mean...

lines.x: Looks like a pretty lively conversation.
I hope I didn't miss too much.
We don't know you.
Really?
How sad.
One Siestan Iced Tea, please.
For this gentleman.
Burp...
What's the matter?
He's shivering. Must be the air conditioning in the bar.
Have another drink, it'll warm you up.
Er, no...
Oh, I forgot. Your tastes have changed.
Your favorite drink was Siestan Iced Tea, back when you could still hold a scalpel. Strong enough to make you forget Loken Williams's abuse.
Now, he has a hundred and twenty-two years left on his prison term, while you... you're free.
Free... you're talking to me about free?
I've been uploading my biometric data on a monthly basis, just like you told me...
Shh.
Let's not talk about work here.
We come to the bar to relax, don't we?
Or would you rather drink with these two? One whose face shows no expression, the other whose face can't be seen at all... than with your old friend?
Old friend? Hah, that's rich.
The reason I've become this, it's all because...
Here's to freedom.
They left me no choice, Saria.
You're on your own.
The man picks up the glass and gulps down its contents.
He stares into space with a particular expression. It is hard to say whether it's one of anger, or one of despair.
A moment later, he falls from his chair.
His legs spasm as though shocked by electricity, while his left hand grasps at his right arm.
His fingers dig deep into his own flesh, as though trying to rip out the electronics that have caused him so much grief, along with his past sins.
Oh my god. What happened to him?
Call an ambulance!

opts: Is he sick?
Something in the drink?

lines.x: Epilepsy.
Blood test shows abnormal chemical composition.
That injection I gave him will help. He'll live.
That drink...
There's half a glass left... I'm not the type to waste.
You drank the rest?
As you just saw.
Still don't get it? Check out your friend's teeth.
Second molar on the left.
A false tooth.
He did one final operation on himself before he left Loken Watertank.
You knew it all along.
I should have removed the variable myself. One tooth wouldn't have affected his work.
But I didn't.
I believe we in Columbia should have the freedom to embrace death.
Doctor—
Take the patient, and give us some space.

opts: On it.
What are you...?

lines.x: We have a problem and we need to deal with it.
Sir, the new test results are out!
Let's hope it justifies this commotion.
Sixty-seven percent?
W-We tried our best.
What about the retests?
H-Here they are.
Get me all the numbers.
Yes... sir.
Sir...
I'll be damned, the old goat wasn't playing.
It's all up to Ho'olheyak now... she'd better take care of Saria.
I'm honored that the former head of Rhine's Defense Section thinks I'm a problem.
Who are you?
We've never met, Director Saria.
But I've heard of you.
One of the co-founders of Rhine Lab, graduate of the Trimounts Institute of Technology, who left academia after earning her PhD in biomedical engineering to become the head of the Defense Section.
Everyone associates Rhine Lab with Kristen Wright, but few have heard of you.
Have you never resented that, after all these years?
That's unnecessary.
I made my choice, I took up my duty.
I know.
You think yourself a firefighter, but what you really love is the feeling of charging into a fire, am I right?
You like fighting.
That doesn't mean you're a warrior. You have no thirst for challenge and destruction.
That's why you never fit in with the scientific community.
Five years ago, you received a grafted plant from Muelsyse, who taught the vines to dance.
You accepted the gift. Thereafter, you were in your office by seven every morning, trimming the sprouts that had grown overeager.
You've been spying on Rhine Lab?
Maybe I just heard some office gossip.
Then maybe you missed the part where Muelsyse turned the offices of three directors into Sargon rainforests.
Or how much trouble it caused Defense.
Well then...
How much trouble did the Diαbolic business cause you?
For all the garbage you've talked, there's one thing you got right.
Hm?
I don't shy away from a fight.
Beautiful Arts.
Did you use that trick against Control?
You're too curious for a mercenary.
Hey, they're fighting!
Are they drunk? Where's a bouncer when you need one?
Are you the bouncer? Get them out of here!
Sorry, I'm just a mechanist.
Hitting the road would be the smart choice here.
Shit! The ceiling's coming down! Go! Go!
Are you okay, Doctor?

opts: I'm fine.
I'm more worried about the informant.
Go help Saria, Mechanist.

lines.x: So eager to stop the fight...
Won't you stay a while longer?
There aren't many in Columbia who could take a hit from you and stay standing.
With skills like yours, there aren't many who could afford your fee.
This doesn't have much to do with Volvort Kochinski. And your style isn't military.
You're eager to kill the trail of the silver reagent.
Your employer could be Ferdinand, or it could be...
...Control?
Even now, you still won't turn your suspicions towards her?
Too many questions to let you go.
Saria!
Saria takes note of her position and that of her colleague. The enemy's path of retreat is cut off.
There is no need to drag this out. She unleashes her Arts without hesitation.
An enamel wall appears out of thin air, and Saria takes aim at Ho'olheyak's vitals.
Looks like the time for talk is over.
You're not dodging?
Don't need to. I'm not in the habit of taking long shots.
Saria's fingers should have found her opponent's abdomen.
But she does not feel the warm touch of soft flesh.
There is no sign of pain on Ho'olheyak's face.
She leans forward, her lips nearly touching Saria's ear.
Believe it or not, Saria, I just wanted to chat.
I'm curious about you... about Rhine Lab.
You hurt my friend the moment you showed up. Doesn't exactly put me in a chatting mode.
Hehe... I have a job to do, after all.
One last parting word for you.
Don't be too eager to make an enemy of me.
Don't be too eager to make an enemy of anyone.
She got away? Why... is there a hole in the wall?
That was me.
Did her Arts misdirect you?
It was some technique, but not Originium Arts.
She changed the air composition around her, interfering with my Arts, and causing my attack to miss its target.
I've never seen anything like it before.

opts: Muelsyse...
Could she keep a caster from getting at water molecules?

lines.x: Muelsyse's disappearance may be her work too.
Maybe.
I've thought about it. Heating air in a specific direction is pretty mundane. It wouldn't have been enough to disable Muelsyse.
Doctor, I propose we go after her.
Whether it's the intel she has access to, or the strange techniques she uses...
This Liberi is extremely dangerous.
Officer? Where are you going?
I didn't know I had to report my every move to you.
Records show you took the key to a vehicle...
Just borrowing an asset.
By all means, report it to your miserable director.
Officer...
What is it? I don't have all day.
Mary Banner. You grew up in the town of Steelham, north of Trimounts. Sonny Romano was your neighbor and middle school classmate.
You've been investigating me?
Romano was registered as Infected four years ago. He ended his internship at a law firm and returned to Steelham. His insurance payments stopped six months later.
His name resurfaced on the crew manifest for this Pioneer Team, three years and seventeen days later.
Where was he during that time?
How would I know?
Preventing problems before they occur is part of our job.
If you thought you could scare me by raffling off some old records, think again.
It took me five years to get to where I am now. Did you think I don't know how to deal with some corporate worms?
Ahem. I have never doubted your ability.
Then don't make me feed you to the tuskbeasts.
Maybe you don't believe it, but I just wanted to give you some friendly advice.
You remind me of my former supervisor at times. I had a lot of respect for her, but she allowed her personal sentiments to destroy her future.
I don't want to see you repeat her mistakes.
Ha... sentiments?
Maybe this could save some people...
But it can't save one out to get herself killed.
This is an emergency communication.
Paging Director Clooney.
Log 17. Location, zone 9 corridor.
No change observed in target's position from the previous log.
Base particle structure is stable.
Still no signal. Can't get anything out.
(We can't tolerate any more delays. The plan needs adjustment...)
(I have to get these reports to the boss once we have comms back.)
...Elena?
*cough* *cough*
What are you doing here?
I... I just wanted to move a little, catch my breath.
I understand that you harbor certain feelings towards the Pioneers... but it's better to stick together.
The last attack was ten minutes ago, not far from here.
Getting separated would be dangerous, if those things appear again.
Alright, I'll go back.
One second.
Before you do... don't you have anything to say to me?
I don't know what you're talking about.
The Pioneers are not in the most stable condition. Angering them will put Joyce in danger.
I understand why you didn't want to tell the truth in front of them.
But it's just the two of us here.
You wanted to ask about the experiments?
You're not a project member. You'll need to request permission from the company...
Stop dodging the question.
I know you're hiding a lot from me.
I'm not angry. We're friends, and we're colleagues. Rhine Lab has strict rules on confidentiality, and all of us are used to hiding our opinions, rules or no rules.
Olivia, I'm not...
You're not doing it on purpose.
I know. How could I not?
There aren't many Infected in the company. When you first came and greeted me at lunch... an Energy Section colleague pulled you aside and whispered to you that I was Infected.
You visited with me anyway.
I don't care about those stones on your body. Who doesn't have a flaw or two? Not the ones gossiping behind your back, at the very least.
Even afterwards... I never cared about the stones in my own body!
That's the Elena Urbica I know.
You knew something was not right with the experiments here.
You knew what those silver things were.
They're endangering our lives, and those of many innocents outside.
The Elena I know would not let something like this happen.
What made you keep quiet?
Did Ferdinand...
Olivia... this is my job.
Just like Helen and so many other researchers who work for Rhine Lab... this is where my dreams can come true.
You said you were once thankful to Director Parvis and Rhine Lab. I...

lines.st: Recite the star chart.
I don't want to see your physics test scores. Full marks or zero, they don't matter.
You're an Urbica. Studying such uselss things insults the name.
You disappoint me, Astgenne.
This is all you have to show for a month's work?
You have talent. But neither does anyone else in Columbian science.
You have determination, but it has yet to be tested.
You're diligent, but there's no direction to guide your hard work.
The world is waiting for you to change it. Every chance you waste is potentially a massive loss for yourself, and the era you're living in.
I chose you from thousands of candidates. You had better not prove me wrong.

lines.x: Ferdinand is the goal I strive for.
What did he promise you? Your own project if you help Director Franks complete this experiment? Or...
Olivia... you're like my sister, treating me like a little kid sitting around waiting for candy.
I know you have a lot of misconceptions about Director Clooney. Your investigations over the years have caused him a lot of trouble.
But none of that matters, it's not my place to defend him.
But he could have refused Rhodes Island's intervention. And yours.
He wants to change Rhine Lab, change Columbia... and I believe all he needs is a chance.
You trust Ferdinand that much?
Did you know that Rhine...
Who's there?
Did those things catch up to us?
It's the Pioneer Team!
Stay back!
The captain was right... we're just lab beasts to you...
You've got it all wrong...
Still trying to trick us?
I won't fall for that again! Everything you did for us, it's all fake!
Calm down, er... Myers, was it?
Myers, if you won't listen to us, maybe you'll listen to Sonny.
I don't... I don't want to die here...
There's no end to this damned corridor... it bears down on you and chokes you out. It's like the walls in the Infected containment zones.
Let us go... please... I'm begging you...
I... I'll kill her if you don't!
Joyce!
How could this be...?
No, let Joyce go... don't hurt her!
The tracks show she passed through here.
Can you tell which way she went?
The alley up ahead.
Let's go.

opts: Wait.
We have unanswered questions.

lines.x: Did you learn anything new, Doctor?

opts: Did we win that fight?

lines.x: I'm not concerned about the outcome of a single battle.
...I get what the Doctor is saying.
That Liberi isn't strong enough to beat us.
But she's not so weak that she couldn't hold out for a few minutes.

opts: She could have gotten away before Mechanist arrived.
Seems like she was waiting for Mechanist and I to arrive.
Seems like she intended to leave a message behind.

lines.x: She's luring us into the alley.
Should we still go?

opts: Yes.

lines.x: We're ready.
On it.
They'll be coming, right?
I know Saria. She'll come, even if she knows it's exactly what I want.
I'm still a little uneasy... that power armor...
Are you sure we don't need permission from Ferdinand?
We could always go back to plan A, if you want.
You mean... never mind, forget that I said anything.
This is a test.
Our employer wants to see our results.
I hear footsteps.
Alert the recon team. Begin the operation.
What about us?
We're out.

lines.st: The cramped space is filled with black, which blocks out light, air and sound.
The mouth cannot scream.
The scorching heat of Originium blocks the windpipe, preventing even a whisper from escaping.
There is no fighting.
Cold hard steel clamps down on the limbs, preventing any attempt at movement, never mind resistance.
If only tears would flow from the eyes. Mother always said tears were powerful weapons.
But the eyes are not cooperating.
They have seen too many mountains fall and too many rivers dry up over the centuries. They have seen too many loved ones die.
They have become dry, like the land after a nomadic city has passed through.

lines.x: All six power armors disabled.
Is that the last one?
Looks like it. We were delayed thirteen minutes.
Wait.
Scans show a seventh!
It's headed your way, Doctor!
I'll take care of it.
You did not see how Saria moved. All you know is that she was standing in front of you before you knew it.
You know she never carries a Rhine-made shield on the streets of Columbia.
She turns herself into a shield.
The enamel barrier growing from her crossed forearms blocks the power armor's attack.
Pest.
You hear Saria mutter under her breath.
That is how she views such rogue Columbian technology.
Her expression tells you what her next move is.
Saria wastes no time dealing with pests.
Her hand closes upon the chest of the power armor, where a dark hole would soon appear.
The power armor would be destroyed, or more aptly, die.
A thought comes to your mind.

opts: Saria, no!

lines.x: She may or may not have heard more than the first syllable, but she does, in fact, stop.
It is the result of trust that you have accumulated over the course of many battles.
Her fist only scrapes the core, but the shockwave is enough to shatter the power armor's outer shell.
You see something amongst the flying metal shards that looks different.
It looks like... strands of hair.
Doctor! Look ou—
It's...
The power armor tips over.
As it further disintegrates, you see more hair, then a speck of skin, then an eye.
The eye is looking at you.
You see many emotions flash across it: anger, fear, bewilderment...
As the shell falls away completely, you realize it is begging for help.
You extend your hand.

opts: Hello, Muelsyse.

lines.x: What's happening, Myers?
I... I don't know, sir...
Let Doc Moore go.
Doc...
I can't.
Disobeying a direct order?
Why are you on their side, sir?
Didn't you say we needed to harden our hearts and do what we had to do? Didn't you say we were just their test subjects?
This was all your idea. I... I liked Doc Moore.
I never thought you'd be the one to turn against me first.
Sam, maybe, or somebody else... but you, Myers, you always listened to me.
To a fault, you said.
I listened to my boss at the plant and ended up with Oripathy. Then I started listening to you when I got here.
You're all smart, got an education. Figured I couldn't go wrong listening to you.
But now... we're all gonna die.
The whitecoats are gonna kill us, Sonny! They think they got it all under control!
Search her if you don't believe me. I'm damned sure she's got a voice recorder on her. She's here to log us, and the silver monsters.
Dr. Urbica, if you know a way out of this, it's time to get talking.
Elena...
Olivia, I may not have told you the details of the experiment...
But I'll never lie to you.
I never lied to you either, Sonny, Myers.
I... I don't know how to deal with them.
I studied them and recorded their behavior because I wanted to solve the problem we're facing... like I said, it's part of my job.
Hm...
I think Elena's telling the truth, Myers.
Would you give Joyce back to us?
Ha... haha....
The Pioneer stares at Ptilopsis. Suddenly, he raises his hand.
Silence lets out a mute cry. Her drone rams the Pioneer, sensing its master's will.
But all he did was take something from Ptilopsis's hand.
It was the treatment band that he put on her only a few hours ago.
What... what have I done...?
Doc Moore...
You regret it, huh? The kindness you showed me?
Someone like me doesn't deserve kindness.
The doctor returns to the side of her friends, still quiet, beautiful, pristine.
But there is no way out. This lab is a tomb, built specially for them.
There is no hope. The band is like a handcuff the company has shackled them with.
They will never find freedom, unless...
The Pioneer stuffs the medical wristband into his mouth.
Myers!
S-Sir...
What are you doing! Spit it out, now!
The band...
It contains multiple doses of painkillers.
His heart can't possibly take it. It'll kill him!
I've seen guys swallow these...
They're all dead!
The dead are the lucky ones...
Spit it out, damn you!
I'll hold his jaw. Sock him in the stomach, Sam!
*cough* *cough*
One more time! I see the treatment band!
Olivia, let's help—
...Olivia?
...Joyce.
—Program start.
She's awake?!
It's so dark...
Why is it so dark outside...?
Dark? But all the lights are on.
Joyce... are your eyes okay?
...Light.
Light... need light... warmth...
Forward...
Fearless...
Can you hear us?
She can't.
What's going on?
I don't know, but none of that sounds like Joyce.
Her condition...
I know her. She's never sounded like this, before or after the operation.
You're right.
You're surprised. You don't know what's happening to her.
That means the situation has long gone out of your control.
Elena... it's time to talk about these experiments.
Did Dorothy...?
I don't want to see anyone get hurt because of our work. Especially not Joyce.
Maybe you're right. We should have worked together to solve the problem.
It's ok, Elena. No need to hesitate anymore.
I invited all of you here. You have the right to know the truth, both Dr. Silence, and my Pioneer friends.
You were... spying on us?
I was concerned.
Someone needs to close the doors while you stop to talk.
You stopped the silver monsters?
Don't call them that, Sonny.
They can hear you.
Elena, my dear assistant, there's one more thing that I require of you.
Bring them to me.
What you don't feel comfortable explaining, I can.
And you know my lab is the safest.
No hostiles left outside.
They all followed Mr. Sonny and Dr. Silence.
Communications are still out.
Looks like jamming across all standard frequencies. Mechanist might have been able to do something about it...
Who's there?
Mr. Sonny?
Freeze.
That voice...
Miss Mary?
Looks like you've got a decent memory.
Why... why are you here?
Where is he?
He... you mean Mr. Sonny?
Tell him to show himself and stop hiding behind a child like a coward!
Er, I'm a grown man...
Just barely.
Out with it, boy. Where are they?
The Pioneers are in a building nearby...
Do you know your ears twitch when you're embarrassed?
Er... huh?
Just kidding.
But you're a little too trusting, aren't you?
This will go a lot easier if you tell me everything you know.
Otherwise, since you're the one who fixed the power supply, I can arrest you for obstruction of justice.
If I can't catch the real perp, I guess you'll have to do.
No, please... don't...
I-I don't want to cause trouble for the Doctor.
Then what are you waiting for? Start talking.
I'm not sure if you'll believe me...
We were attacked by a bunch of silver shapeshifters, the likes of which we've never seen before.
So, she's inside.
Yes. This is Dorothy's lab, and the heart of the complex.
You sure?
What's the matter, Sonny?
Nothing.
It's wicked stressful, getting so close to the truth.
Remember what I said, Sam. Be ready.
Yes, sir.
Can you open the door, Dr. Urbica?
Of course.
(Elena.)
(Huh?)
(Something feels off with the Pioneers.)
(But we've come this far.)
(You convinced me that we're all here to solve the same problem, Olivia.)
(I'm not so sure anymore...)
Clock's ticking, Doctor.
Same here.
Come in, please.
Elena's hand never touched the scanner.
But the doors of the lab open to the true master of the house.
Hello, everyone.
I'm the Director of Rhine Lab's Originium Art Section, and the overseer of Test Site 359.
Welcome, all, to my laboratory.
So we're here at last...
Sam. Get her!
Muelsyse...
Urgh... nngh...
I never expected to see you here.
Sorry.
Sorry...?
I would have killed you if the Doctor didn't warn me.
Is that what the enemy wanted? Did they want you to murder your former colleague?
Not necessarily.
It wasn't just because of the Doctor that I stayed my hand.
She said, 'Don't be too eager to make an enemy of anyone.' She was alluding to Muelsyse.

opts: That would explain her behavior.
She was guiding us to Muelsyse.

lines.x: She wanted us to find her?
But one wrong move, and we might've killed Muelsyse...
Alright, I get it. She doesn't care if Muelsyse lives or dies.

opts: She's treating this as a test.
She wants Saria to act quicker.

lines.x: A test of the power armor technology, and a test to see how Rhodes Island and I react.
Every word she said in the bar was calculated to probe my response.
She's guiding us in the direction that she wants.
Ugh...!
Feeling better, Muelsyse?
You were injected with a sedative a few hours ago, but you'd regained consciousness by the time we saw you in the power armor.
The nausea and numbness that you feel are mainly lingering effects of hypoxia and extreme stress.
I patched you up a little, but it'll take you another half hour or so to fully recover.
Stop... stop...
Stop?
Stop... who?
The one who put you in the power armor?
The Liberi?
No...
Muelsyse begins sweating from her brow and the back of her hand.
She grabs your hand, and the drops of sweat rise into the air, splashing against your clothing.
The stain quickly spreads to form a message.
It is not the name of the Liberi, nor of Ferdinand.
Site 359.
Not often I see you here.
You did give me a temporary work permit, didn't you?
'Bibliology consultant'. What's a tech firm like Rhine need with that?
You're in a good mood. I take it your experiments are going well?
Don't talk about experiments right now.
Ten minutes. A man like me only gets ten minutes to himself a day.
I could spend it on a video call with the children, or dinner with Elena and the others.
But I prefer to spend it doing nothing but standing here and watching.
It's just an ordinary street out there.
Yes. It's everything.
They must be tired after a long day of work. Why is there no sign of dejection on their faces? Why are their steps so springy?
Because they have a chance.
They believe their hard work is giving them a chance at better lives for them and their families.
Just like those Pioneers outside the city.
Yes, them too.
Trimounts. The city of three burdenbeasts.
Three burdenbeasts carried seven Pioneers to build the first settlement on this soil.
And now? Look at the streets below.
The Pioneers and their descendants bring a constant stream of vitality to this place. No other country can claim to rival Columbia's enterprising spirit.
Our country's prosperity has given people confidence, which in turn feeds into our vitality.
If the country keeps advancing, so will the lives of everyone across all the lands.
I know what you think of me. You, Kristen, Saria... you call me an opportunist.
It takes a smart man to take a chance when he comes across it.
Thank you.
I can't deny that I would rather be up here than down there.
But I've earned the chances I've been given.
My projects have generated wealth, which fed into the next project, and driven technological progress.
We stand on the cutting edge, leading Terra into a new era.
They will build monuments, and our names will be immortalized upon them.
Our?
Victory belongs to each and every trailblazer.
My ten minutes are almost up.
You didn't give me any time to talk.
I know what you came for, Ho'olheyak.
Oh?
I forgive you.
Kristen, Muelsyse, Saria, Parvis... and you. I'm not wasting my time trying to guess your petty little thoughts.
Dorothy's experiments are one step away from success.
All I care about is the final result.
S-Sonny...
Dorothy says we're safe here... why do you have your crossbows out?
Safe?
You're not stupid, Doctor.
The biggest danger is right there.
...Dorothy?
I thought you were willing to hear us out?
I'm sorry.
Out of the way, you and the other docs. We're not here for you.
But...
We've been tricked, Elena.
Stopping the attack on Joyce was just a show.
He showed us his anger, his hesitation, and his bottom line, all of this to hide one thing.
He was always prepared to commit a crime.
I didn't think it'd be Myers. The whole part of making you trust me... especially Dr. Urbica... I was gonna make Sam play the bad guy.
But that's all behind us now.
We're here, right at the source.
You planned the kidnapping and came all the way here to kill me?
May I ask... why?
A voice so soft it could melt the hardest heart.
Watery eyes opened slightly wide.
Filled with innocence, surprise, even hurt.
As though it was those who picked up arms to defend themselves who should be reproached.
Why? You can still ask why?
I saw the whole it all.
The monsters you made, stalking us. How you fed them the blood of my friends.
You... evil...
There are no monsters!
Those are byproducts of the experiments, made of a special material that's sensitive to neural signals...
No, Elena. He's not making a blind guess.
It really does seem like he's seen those silver things before.
And more.
Yeah.
Three days ago, I snuck into the lab complex to find you.
I wanted to ask if I could volunteer to be a test subject.
Sir! Didn't you say it was too risky? That the corps could be lying to us?
Risky? Haha... hahaha!
Sorry, Sam. I was the one lying.
But I wanted to fix my mistakes.
Listen, everybody.
I saw silver monsters taking shape around her that night. She talked to them like they were her pets.
Dorothy Franks is the one behind all this.
Do you find it hard to accept what I'm telling you?
Can't say I'm into sci-fi.
Then maybe you could take a look at this letter.
Letter?
Mr. Sonny gave it to me. He might've known he'd be in danger.
He said to me, and I quote, 'Give this letter to Mary Banner if anything happens to me.'
I'm sure he wouldn't mind you reading it... a little early.
Give it to me.
Er... what does it say?
Where the hell did that guy go?!
Er...
You don't know Sonny Romano, Greyy.
He's a ball of anger and hate, all over his insurance.
Insurance again...
Yes, every Infected in Columbia deals with that word a lot.
People outside think Columbia is kind enough to the Infected.
You get to live a normal life, as long as you can pay for your health insurance. That sounds pretty darned fair, doesn't it?
That's what Sonny told me when he first got sick.
But where did that end up?
Was it... very expensive?
On a cop's salary? I couldn't afford it, even if I quit eating altogether.
It wasn't meant to ensure keeping the Infected healthy. The premiums are way out of line with the treatment they actually get.
It's more like a fine. Or a little trick that forces the Infected to down a certain path.
Sonny Romano never wanted to face the truth.
If he's not stopped, he could end up going down a path you don't come back from.
What you saw were neither monsters nor pets.
But they do have something to do with my experiments.
So you admit it.
The truth will do you no good.
The more you know, the more danger you're in.
People up high always like to make decisions for the little guy.
Why don't you put down those pointy things? My instruments can be fragile.
Or if you have to aim, aim this way.
I don't mind.
She takes a step towards the Pioneers, almost touching the crossbows.
Sonny's hand trembles ever so slightly. He forces himself to look away from that warm pair of eyes.
This is a ploy. A trick to get them to submit again.
Aren't you afraid to die?
I am.
But I'm more afraid that you'll hurt yourselves.
You're not a murderer, Sonny, and neither are your friends. You're just ordinary men, trying to live better lives.
Didn't you consider participating in my experiments?
You still have a chance. Join us, and you'll get what you've always wanted.
I'll never turn you down.
She extends a hand to the Pioneers, as though waiting for them to clasp it.
Does she not understand that they came to kill her?
No. Don't look at her. Her eyes, her hands, they're too soft and warm.
They, the people who walk the barrenlands, have never known anything but cold refusal from the cities and those who live in them.
She is controlling them, like she controlled the others whose wills were weak.
But, the word 'chance'...
When was the last time we heard it?
Hm...
It's those silver constructs!
Where did they come from? I didn't see...
Sonny... was right.
This lab is where they were born.
They're protecting Dorothy.
Stop. Don't hurt each other...
It's coming!
More over there!
Look out!
Grab Dorothy Franks—no, destroy the lab!
We gotta destroy this cradle of evil to stop the monsters, once and for all!
No!
That is the one thing that I will not permit.
The experiments must continue.
It was my promise to you... to them.
Here, the data you asked for.
Thanks, Ferdinand. I didn't think you'd hand it over personally.
We were able to retrieve most of the Loken Watertank data thanks to you. The fewer that know about this, the better.
And I'm curious as to why you wanted these data.
She gazes at the monitor in front of her, so focused that even her breathing slows down.
A video of a girl with white hair is playing.
The girl spends most of her time sleeping, eating, or sitting in a corner with a blank look on her face.
From time to time, she is led into a lab, where she crushes furniture placed in a room separated by a 1-meter thick wall.
What a beautiful child.
You remind me of Loken Williams. I thought only madmen boast about their test subjects.
Sure, he's been accused of crossing a moral line.
Morals are shackles that the short-sighted impose upon themselves.
Science has never been bound by common sense. There is no greater insult to a scientist than to be judged by moral standards.
Williams's problem was that he allowed the infinite unknown to get the better of his sanity, and he abandoned the logic and reason a scientist must grip.
See at the end of the video how your 'beautiful child' turned the lab into a nightmarish catastrophe.
The monitor is showing a different image.
There is no recorded sound, but the screams seem to pierce the screen and fill a lab from another time.
The fallen debris does not hide the bloodstains.
What happened to the girl?
Records pertaining to her have been tampered with. Like the other legacies of Loken Watertank, she has value to somebody, somewhere.
I want to find her.
May I assume you're considering ending Williams's experiments?
She is too difficult to control. A risk Rhine Lab cannot afford.
She didn't volunteer to be a test subject. I can't ask something so cruel of her.
You want to heal her?
We're not doctors, but anyone whose seen the experiments in their entirety should not harbor such delusions.
No, Ferdinand.
All I want to do is find her... and give her a hug.
A... a hug?
The girl... she's all alone.
They took her family from her, and made her something entirely different from all other people.
I can't help but think... where is she now?
Has she made new friends? Or new enemies?
I want to hear her voice...
I can't change her past, but the future may not be lost yet.
You know, Dorothy... I really have no idea what you're thinking at times.
Not many scientists are as sentimental as you are. You're not bound by logic or reason either. Perhaps, one day, your madness will put Williams's to shame...
Madness? I've seen this in my dreams, many times.
That's why I want to find the girl.
If she's still alive... if anyone can save her from her past...
Then maybe someone will be there to stop me, if I really do go over the edge.
'Hello, Mary. When was the last time I wrote to you? I'm afraid I was never able to muster the courage to speak to you after the incident.
I could not sleep at all last night. Memories from childhood kept intruding into my mind.
Remember when you were fifteen, and we climbed up the judge statue in the center of town?
You joked that it was illegal, and that you would arrest me when you became a cop.
And I... what did I say?
'Everyone has the right to enjoy fairness and justice.'
I could not have imagined what would become of me in just a few years.
Civilization is a fragile thing. Not much separates us from the burdenbeasts outside the nomadic cities.
Fairness and justice have become alien concepts. All I think of, day in, day out, is how to survive.
Yes, I have become the kind of man you most despise. The kind that I used to most despise.
(Signs of erasure)
What if I have the chance to return to the embrace of civilization? To go back to the man I used to be?
Your birthday is just a week away. You have no idea how much I would love to surprise you.
(Long blank, then hastily scribbled words)
They're performing dangerous experiments on us. All I can do is try to keep living.'
S-Sir. Our bolts...
They... stopped in mid-air?
The bolts headed towards her and the lab equipment are frozen in mid-air.
It is as though they are being held by countless invisible hands.
Dorothy raises a hand.
She touches one of the bolts with her finger.
It gives off a light hum.
Hit the deck!
What the...?
There is a loud bang, like a heavy metal object being dropped to the floor.
The bolts have fallen to the ground—but only part of them. The heads of the bolts have disappeared, leaving behind only an eerily smooth cross-section.
No, they did not disappear.
Did you hear that, Elena?
It sounded like...
Like metal vibrating at an extremely high frequency.
The heads of the bolts shook themselves apart.
How powerful could Director Franks's Originium Arts be?
It's not all Arts.
It's her technique. She's THE authority on applied Originium Arts.
The Pioneers won't be able to lay a finger on her.
You're right. There's someone else we should be worried about...
Oww...
Take care of Joyce. She's still in bad shape.
Not to mention Sonny and his men...
Firebomb!
On it!
Don't aim, just throw!
Good god...
Why... why won't you listen?
I don't want to see you hurt.
Why don't you tell that to the men you tricked onto your lab table?
I didn't trick them.
They're safe. Very safe.
If the experiment succeeds, they'll never have to face the dangers of the wastes again.
Isn't that what you wanted, Sonny?
Watch out!
Sam...?
Sir... Sonny, run...
Take the others and get out...
Out to the barrenlands... where the corporations can't find you.
The barrenlands...
Yes... we're Pioneers...
Ha... haha! They're afraid of the wastes... we're not!
Run?
Turn, and run from your friends, run from your answers.
Run away and live. Isn't that what I've always wanted?
What's that, Sonny... a letter? Someone back in town waiting for you to write? Man, you lucky bastard.
Maybe it'll become evidence.
Evidence?
To let the people out there know what happened to us.
I thought we were just gonna save Dirk and the others.
What if we fail?
I wanna leave behind something, Sam. If we don't make it, I want people to know that we tried.
He wants to live.
But more than that, he wants someone to hear his voice.
Why do we have to wait for you to give us a chance?
That's not fair.
He finds one of the bolts on the ground.
It's missing the head, but he still has his hands.
He's lost much to Oripathy. But he also gained a little bit—a sliver of power.
In the grand scheme of things, that power can't make a difference. Here and now, though, it is enough to snap the bolt in half.
The sharp cross-section could be a weapon against the enemy before him, to break the shackles imposed upon him.
Don't come any closer.
Grr...
He loses control of the bolt he's holding, then his hands themselves.
A powerful but invisible force grabs his wrists, forcing him to stop.
My research was never intended to be used against you.
It was designed to help you deal with the beasts of the wastes, overcome harsh terrain, and survive Catastrophes. To give you a chance.
It's supposed to protect you!
Hah... protect us?
Did you ever ask what we wanted?
He will move forward. The more it is denied him, the more he will struggle.
The broken bolt tears through his skin, causing blood to drip from it.
Won't you just let this go?
Ha... haha!
No.
No, I don't wanna.
What's the situation at the site?
Defense says no one has left it.
If all goes according to plan, Dorothy's 'Hub' should be complete by now.
Any news from Elena?
Negative.
As of the last communication, she had not arrived at the observation station. Defense staff have not seen her.
Go and prepare the latest batch of Transmitter.
How much do you need?
One-third.
Time is running out for Rhine Lab.
The experiments are going well, Colonel. We're in the final phase.
We can't wait much longer.
The aftershocks of the Victorian Incident continue to reverberate. Both our friends and our enemies are making preparations.
We believe a weapon like that remains in the hands of someone, somewhere.
A century of hard work could vanish in the blink of an eye if they target one of our cities.
We'll be dragged back under the dark clouds, at the mercy of other nations.
That's why we need more advanced technology... the technology to deliver our weapons to any location with precision, regardless of distance.
Yes. Columbia needs a 'guide'.
You're smart, Clooney, and you're more practical than most smart people.
When this deal is done, I look forward to a fruitful partnership with the new Control of Rhine Lab.
The feeling is mutual.
Send more hands to the labs to package the reagents.
Yes, I need all of it.
Whew... these guys are a headache.
You've seen my Arts. They're nowhere near as practical as Greyy's.
Yes, we've proven that their liquid structure is extremely resilient.
And there is worse news.
Did I mention that there's more of this material stored above us, all over the lab complex? Like, ten thousand times more, give or take.
Maybe you should have mentioned that sooner.
You said they were byproducts of the experiments. What was the main objective?
A new technology that would replace traditional Arts Units.
Energy was responsible for developing this special material. It's extremely sensitive to microvibrations, making it the ideal medium for receiving and encoding neural signals.
We call it 'Transmitter'.
Under ideal circumstances, it can turn someone with ordinary Originium Arts assimilation into an outstanding caster.
That's why... even the byproduct... can be very... dangerous!
Olivia, I can't fight and give a lecture at the same time.
Sometimes I wish I had one of those devices like in Joyce's brain. That way, I could tell you stuff with just a thought.
Well then, let me ask another question.
How do we stop this experiment?
I know you put a lot into it, and I understand your loyalty to Dorothy. But...
Enough, Olivia, you're reminding me of my sister again.
The project is important to me, but something's clearly not right with Dorothy... did you think I was the kind of person who can't get my priorities straight?
I... just... need... to... think.
I haven't spent enough time here. I wish I pestered Dorothy more...
...The caster.
What?
Elena, if the things before us are products of Originium Arts, where is the caster?
It's not Dorothy controlling them. They're just there by her side.
The missing Pioneers...
The one place in the lab that Dorothy is most hesitant to show us.
Use your Originium Arts, Elena! Aim behind her!
Max power, right?
Starlight rains from the sky, catching everybody's attention for an instant.
Even the strange silver entities.
No, not there!
You shouldn't have done that, Elena.
I... I can't stop... what's going on?
It's like my limbs aren't my own... how much is that equipment worth? Never mind... got to hold on...
It's not just us. Even the Pioneers...
An earthquake?
Hang in there!
Star... stars...
Joyce... Olivia... I can't keep my hold on her...
I'm losing sensation in my hands!
A-Arts...!
Never thought the day would come when I would be giving myself electric shocks...
Electric?
Electricity can interfere with Originium Arts effects...
Did you say that this research is focused on vibration?
Yeah... and?
Sand.
Sand's everywhere in the lab. On us, too... so small we never even noticed.
Keep looking for the test subjects, Elena!
I'll stop Dorothy.
Sir, the reagents have been loaded.
Good. We're going to Site 359.
You're going there personally?
Did I not make myself clear?
Er, I just thought there were enough staff there already...
Tell me, why is it that the more important an experiment is, the more times it has to be repeated?
Er... to avoid errors?
Because an important result must be absolutely reliable.
Is Director Franks not reliable?
Dorothy? She is driven by her emotions, and achieved great things because of them.
But her emotions may also become a weakness, at key moments.
What about Elena?
I hope you grow faster than her.
She is motivated, but too inexperienced. She may not be able to make the best possible decision without my advice.
There's only one person at Rhine Lab whom I trust implicitly—me.
Dr. Silence...
*Cough*... hello, Director Franks.
How did you come this far? I thought the vibration membrane would cut off nerve signals to your limbs.
I'm a physician. I know the human body a little better than you do.
I'm sorry. I know it must be uncomfortable.
The experiment will be complete in just a little bit. I'll set you free when we're done.
And then...?
Is it really worth the damage that's being done?
Look at the people who are struggling right now.
Don't... give... up!
Grab... my... hand!
Those focused on the future tend to ignore what's happening in front of them.
I've seen many people like that at Rhine Lab.
But Director Franks... Dorothy... you're not like them.
There are tears in your eyes when you look at Sonny and the other Pioneers.
I can feel their pain.
But which is worse? A moment of pain, or an eternity of pain?
The life they lead is a quagmire, Dr. Silence.
I want to pull them up, give them the same chances that you and I once had.
A drowning man may struggle even more when he feels someone pulling him.
But is that really him refusing help?
The bog has choked them, but I hear their cries for help. I've extended my hand, and I'm not about to pull it back now.
But you need to stop.
Your drone... when did you...?
While you were talking.
I've seen many places since I left Rhine Lab, some of them even worse than this sand-filled lab of yours.
A physician knows how to keep her equipment clean at all times.
Still...
Dorothy... she won't even think about giving up...
(My hands are getting number.)
(Another shock? I'll probably fry my nerves.)
...Light.
Light? My Arts? You didn't use to love my Arts show this much, Joyce.
Stars... bright...
Far away.
Where are you looking... what are you looking at?
No, what really matters isn't what you're looking at...
It's what you know... what did you learn that Olivia and I didn't?
Home...
In the light.
Huh?
You... you're moving?
Wait, you were unconscious... that's why the vibration membrane didn't affect you as much.
The direction you're pointing...
That's the direction the silver constructs are gathering in.
I know where the missing people went.
Look, a door opened—
That's...
Door... opened?
No, look out—
All the vibrations stop.
The silver constructs dissolve into harmless pools of liquid.
Dorothy too stops dead in her tracks, giving up resistance even with Silence's drone at her neck.
All she sees is the door, and everything behind it.
What she vowed to protect, and the countless dreams that she carries.
The massive machine sits silent in the darkness.
The intertwining tubes are like sprawling rhizomes, shoots growing out at the ends.
Each shoot is an electrode glowing faintly, absorbing nutrition from the lifeform underneath.
The missing Pioneers lie side by side, electrodes on their heads.
They breathe and their hearts beat, but they are oblivious to what is happening around them.
A silvery liquid flows through the tubes, converging on the device at the center of the room.
It seems more alive than the dozens of catatonic test subjects.
How could this be...?
...Helen?
Why...?
Dirk! Gale! Sophia!
They're all here...
Let them go.
Please, keep your voice down. Don't startle them.
Wait here a while, my friends... everything will be alright, once I take care of this little problem. I promise.
Are you talking to them? I doubt they can hear you.
I've never seen symptoms like these... except on the unconscious Joyce.
The electrodes and the silver liquid... they're connected to their central nervous systems, aren't they?
Dorothy... why is Helen here?
She was worried that she would fail Ferdinand's test. I made a suggestion.
A suggestion... to become a test subject?
Helen was chatting with us just this morning. Now she lies here as conscious as a plank of wood.
I want to help her, Elena.
I can feel her pain... just like I feel all of theirs.
Helen's debts are weighing an entire family down.
She lives in constant fear... she would take her own life if Ferdinand fired her.
To stop now would be to deliver them into the hands of merciless fate.
I can't do something so cruel.
Stop.
Stop with the self-righteousness already, or I can't promise I'll keep my finger off the trigger.
Your hatred of me stems from your lack of understanding of what you see, and of me.
But I understand you.
Like hell you do!
You understand us so well you'll oh so benevolently give us a chance at a new life. Instead of dying in a Catastrophe or getting eaten by monsters...
We get to be technically-alive half-corpses in the lab of a big corporation!
Where do I line up to kiss your feet?!
Have you never thought about it, Sonny? Why is it that some people with Oripathy can keep working, while others are forced to leave their homes?
Don't need you to tell me.
The day after I got diagnosed, I found my insurance contract and pink slip waiting for me.
The contract was five hundred and sixty-nine pages. The sheet telling me I was fired didn't fill half the paper.
There aren't a lot of companies out there willing to hire an Infected. We gotta prove we're worth that outrageous insurance premium.
Sigh...
Don't you think it's unfair?
Kinda goes without saying, doesn't it?
Then you should understand.
I can't reach Silence or Greyy.
The site must be entirely closed off.
Fer...dinand...
Closing off the lab is just the means, not the end.
Ferdinand is planning something at Site 359.
Doctor, Silence and the others are in danger.
We're not getting anything from the site... should we move to assist?

opts: Ptilopsis...
...Device #9.

lines.x: True... data transmission from Device #9 is different from standard communication. We only need a moment's signal to see what Ptilopsis is seeing.
I'll try to sync up with her.
Are you sure you want to meet with him alone, Miss Franks?
Yes, I have permission from the bureau.
They say that man, Loken Williams, is a complete nutjob. He got a hundred years in prison for his cruel human experimentation.
I know. I've sat in his lectures before.
These scientists are out of their minds... oh, sorry, I forgot you were a scientist too. But you're such a gentle and elegant lady. Surely you're not like them!
I understand. Thank you for being concerned about my safety. I'll be careful.
How long do you expect you'll be in there?
An hour. I can come back again same time next week, if necessary.
You want to talk to him that badly? He doesn't usually care about other people...
Maybe it's because of all the things people ask him for, I'm the only one asking for help.
Everyone deserves a chance, we should all have equal access to opportunity. Differences in talent should not determine one's destiny.
When someone with talent becomes privileged because of that talent, they are infringing on someone else's interests, undermining fairness.
I'm among the privileged. And I'm ashamed of my 'luck.'
I wanted to pull you out of the bog before it swallowed you—Dirk, Helen, and you, Sonny.
I have done all this in the hopes that one day, fortune will smile only upon those who work hard, rather than favor the so-called 'talented'.
I've spent many years working towards this goal.
Altering the individual to make it stronger does not solve the problem.
Those who were born strong can be even stronger with the same alterations, widening the gap even further.
In order to achieve true equality, I must...
You wanted to eliminate individual differences?
The unconscious test subjects... Originium Arts constructs with emotional responses... a massive machine that links them together...
Your experiments... they're...
I've heard of something like them before, but I thought it was just a mad dream, one that failed a long time ago.
Mad dream?
There's no such thing as 'mad' science. It's just a means to an end.
We can't ignore or forget the innate differences between individuals, but that doesn't mean we have to be burdened by them forever.
My technology will allow people to stand before each other as equals, sharing equally the soil, the air, and the rain.
No longer will their footsteps be stopped by mountains, nor their sight be obstructed by storms.
They will look into the distance from a place of safety, without having to endure the toil of long journeys and separation from their loved ones.
Don't you understand the beauty of such a future?
Did I never actually understand...?
What exactly were we doing here?
We've been looking for the caster behind the silver constructs.
We thought it was the individual Pioneers, but we were wrong.
The caster was all of them.
You have sharp eyes, Dr. Silence.
Director Franks, the future you imagine is just that... your imagination.
I see something different.
A skilled Originium Arts caster can destroy a building. Three skilled casters can suppress an entire city block.
What would happen if you joined the power of ten people, a hundred people, a thousand people?
Nothing under the sun will escape its gaze. The strongest fortress will be little more than a toy that it could crush with a thought.
As for the people you care about, the ones you call friends... they will never be more than test subjects in the eyes of those who want to harness the technology.
Those who fail will be the lucky ones. They'll be ignored, allowed to live and die on their own.
A worse fate befalls those who succeed.
The day their experiment succeeds, they'll be doomed to live under the watchful eyes of corporations and governments.
Olivia... I've never seen you like this...
This angry.
Silence can feel her own anger, like a humming in her mind. Scenes from the past flash across her eyes, as she feels her reason and calm disintegrate.
You may have changed their destiny, but at what price? They'll lose their freedom forever, their lives no longer their own.
...I'll protect them.
If it costs my very life.
Killing you, right here, right now just might be best for everyone.
If that's your consensus...
Then I won't resist.
Don't lose your head, Sonny!
Lose my head, Doc?
I'm calmer than I've ever been.
Dorothy Franks... I gotta admit, the picture of the future you're painting has some appeal.
Same as how I almost walked into your lab... you don't know how many times I thought about taking your hand.
Why didn't you?
Because my future... belongs to me.
The things you say, not to mention the look of you there... the more welcoming the whole deal, the clearer I see it...
You're controlling us.
It's harder to break out of a sweet dream than a nightmare.
Maybe you're living that dream, Director Franks, but the rest of us are here facing reality.
Doc Silence was right. An experiment like this should never have existed to begin with.
The lands across our world have given birth to enough madness. This kinda madness oughta get buried deep, and never dug up again.
Whatever it costs.
We gotta put a stop to this.
Olivia! Make Sonny listen!
Whatever came of it... Dorothy was only trying to help these people.
Even if I thought she needed to be stopped, to be punished, it shouldn't end like this... not by the ones she truly loved...
Sonny...
Joyce?
You... you're awake?
Don't hurt Director Franks.
Synchronization failed.
The situation at Site 359 may be worse than we thought.
Uhh...

opts: Are you alright?

lines.x: Thank you for the help, Dr. Doctor.
I can feel my legs now.

opts: Site 359 is about more than just Rhine Lab.
Ferdinand is working with more powerful forces.

lines.x: You know who Ferdinand is working with, don't you, Saria?
The power armor you've been tracking just might end up in the hands of those who most need cutting-edge weaponry.
You've seen them. Evidence?
You'll have it.
That warmth I felt back there, that was a little wishful thinky on my part, huh? I put my life on the line to get the evidence you wanted.
Our hypothesis was right.
Ferdinand is no longer hiding his ambition.

opts: He's leveraging this experiment to garner support...

lines.x: ...and he'll use it to seize control of Rhine Lab.
We have to find Kristen. She is Ferdinand's prime target.
Did you get my last message, Saria?
My contacts inside Rhine say everything is normal.
That's because they're used to Control's absence.
Ferdinand's been planning this for years, undermining Control's authority, little by little... this experiment is the last step.
Or maybe not the last one...
I hadn't been in contact with Kristen for a week before they got me.
What about the site?
I wouldn't be too worried about Dr. Silence. I still remember that time she got the upper hand of me.
This is no time for indecision, Saria.
You have to make a choice.
This is no multiple choice question, Doctor.
The key to resolving the situation at the site is Ferdinand, and the key to taking care of Ferdinand lies before us.

opts: Let's go see the Control of Rhine.

lines.x: ...Sonny.
We did this of our own volition.
I don't get it, Doc Moore... who are you talking for here?
Message.
I received a message, one that is different from that previous ones.
Hngh...
Does your head hurt again, Joyce?
There is nothing wrong with me physically, Dr. Silence.
Detecting unexpected data transmission. Processing.
Data?
Yes... Sonny, we all know...
This is... a gamble.
The results are... unpredictable. But this was our choice.
Data transmission terminated. Analysis indicates that Sonny is the intended recipient.
Doc Moore, you're talking like one of our... no, one of the test subjects lying there.
Hypothesis cannot be confirmed.
The source of the data is unknown. I simply sensed it.
Doc Silence, is she right? Doc Moore can hear my friends?!
Joyce, was your use of the word 'sense' intentional?
Yes, Dr. Silence.
The content of the dream has changed.
What did you see?
Analysis failed.
Your eyes look different... are you feeling sad?
Detecting signal fluctuations in peripheral system.
...Della.
Dellareed? Are you thinking of her again? Does Device #9 have something to do with Director Franks's experiments?
Motive, emotion, not logical connections.
Dr. Silence, I sense that this was our own choice.
Your own choice?
Sonny, if I guarantee that I will do all that can be done to correct the wrongs, will you let Dorothy Franks go?
Listen, Doc Silence, we appreciate your help getting us here, and that you got us to the truth about the experiments.
We can definitely believe you're on our side.
But can you really be sure she won't start it all back up again?
No, I can't.
But if she does, I will handle her for you.
Whew... the experiments have been suspended, Dorothy is unharmed, and the test subjects are regaining consciousness.
I still find all this hard to believe.
There is no cause for alarm. Emotional fluctuations are normal.
You woke up just in time, Joyce.
I've never seen Olivia that angry... I thought she would just leave Dorothy to her own devices.
But I still don't understand how you heard the voices of the test subjects.
Their consciousnesses were linked by Dorothy's 'Hub', but how did you get involved?
Didn't you say Device #9 had nothing to do with the 'Hub'?
Insufficient data available.
But I heard the voice of Mr. Myers when I woke up from my dream.
When you woke up...
The conflict in the corridor? Why would that wake you, when the battle before didn't?
This is all so strange...
Anyway, I guess all's well that ends well. Thanks to Olivia.
Dr. Silence has met expectations.
She's changed so much, come to think of it.
When I first met her, she was just one of many students you'd see on a college campus, shy, introverted, unremarkable.
This description matches my memory.
I could hardly recognize her when she was pressing Dorothy for answers, only to step in to protect her.
It was like I saw... a shadow of someone else.
That of Director Saria?
H-How did you know?
Don't tell Olivia I said that!
Dr. Silence won't let this affect her friendship with you.
I know.
But as her friend, I don't want to remind her of her unpleasant past.
Understood.
Let's go. I should finish my job too.
Communications have yet to be restored... not good. The boss...
And my sister... how many messages have I been getting?
Please come with me, Director Franks. It's time to leave.
One moment, please.
With the experiments halted, they will wipe this lab off the map.
There's something I have to take with me.
It's... a photo?
Yes.
A photo of a group of Pioneers...
Is this girl... you?
I meant every word I said to Sonny.
My mother, my whole family... they were all Pioneers.
From a young age I heard them talk about their dreams and saw their struggles with my own eyes.
They gave me a chance to chase my own dreams.
But most Pioneers will never know the luck that came to me.
What happened to your family?
Most of them have left me.
When I was invited to attend Ironforge Polytechnic's summer camp for 'talented students'...
A Catastrophe destroyed my mother's camp.
She never heard about my miraculous acceptance, or my scholarship.
I'm sorry...
Was it really the right choice to stop these experiments?
Those Pioneers, those whose lives hang by a thread, they live each day not knowing if they'll see the next.
I could have helped them...
They didn't have to be swallowed up by the sand in despair, like my family was.
Why do you ask this, Silence?
Without our experiments, the child would not have lasted more than a few days after leaving Haydn 1.
We saved her life. She wants for nothing, and will contribute to scientific advancement.
How lucky is she, compared to all those children who die of Oripathy in the barrenlands?
I trusted you, put you in charge of her. Yet you make such petty accusations towards me, towards science itself?
I don't know.
This is not a question for me to answer.
Joyce reminded me...
I should not judge things by my own standards, even if I can never accept what you did.
Did we not choose to be tormented by uncertainty and self-doubt?
As researchers, it's our duty to seek the answer ourselves.
Is everything ready?
Our team has entered the site and is approaching the lab area.
Good. Get ready.
Colonel, I know there have been delays, but if you give me a little more time...
You never told me there were other people at the site, Clooney.
Do you mean the pharmaceutical company?
There's nothing to worry about. They're partnered with Rhine Lab. I will...
I don't need your promises.
All I need here is results.
I apologize. This is just a small hiccup...
If you can't deliver results, then you have to at least stop making trouble for me.
You'd do well to remember that.
Can we proceed, Director Clooney?
We don't have a choice.
Understood.
One more thing.
Put me on spec com.
Huh... communications have been restored?
Elena.
Director?
I'm so glad it's you. I've been trying to reach you... did you get my message? I put everything that happened here in my report.
Something's wrong with Dorothy... Director Franks's experiments. But Silence and I have things under control.
We've settled things with the Pioneers. They're leaving the labs as we speak.
I know.
You... you do? Oh wow...
You did your job. The cleanup detail is on the way in.
Defense? They're finally here?
Whew, that's a relief. I was worried about the Transmitter we've been storing here.
They know what to do. You can leave now.
I should stay and help. Next to Dorothy, I'm the most familiar with these experiments.
I'm at the observation station, Elena. Two directors are more than enough to handle the situation, don't you agree?
Er, that's not what I meant...
Then listen to me and leave the site immediately.
May I ask why?
No, you may not waste my time with meaningless questions. I want you in the control room, now.
Yes... sir.
Is there a problem, Researcher Urbica?
The director... Ferdinand wants me to leave immediately.
Leave?
Go and let Olivia know that the Defense Section is coming.
What about you? Are you leaving?
I... something's not right with the director. If... if he didn't know how far Dorothy went, why is he completely unconcerned about what happened to the experiments?
Director Clooney, Miss Urbica is not leaving the labs.
Should we adjust the plan?
No.
Proceed.
Is everybody out?
Yes, sir.
Next.
What are they doing?
They say it's a 'precautionary measure'. Probably a sedative or something.
I guess we made a little too much noise, huh?
Don't forget what I said. If anyone asks, I was the one behind everything.
Don't worry, no one's letting you take all the credit.
But...
Next.
Sonny Romano.
Wasn't asking for your name.
Tell your leader that I take sole responsibility for the kidnapping.
Right hand.
Are you gonna get to it after the injection?
What the...? Doc Urbica? Why'd you push me?
Don't take the shot!
You're interfering with our work, researcher.
I developed that stuff in that syringe.
Need me to explain what it's for?
Huh? Aren't they with you and Doc Silence?
Why do you always doubt the people you should trust, when you're so eager to trust people you should doubt?
Take that band off, quick!
The treatment wristband?
I remembered why Joyce woke up when I saw the syringes in their hands!
It's this—the treatment bands!
The ones you got have been modified. There's a sensor on them similar to the equipment in Dorothy's lab. If it comes into contact with Transmitter...
I need to report to the director. Someone is behind all this, using... using...
Dammit, communications are down again?
What's the matter?
I... I get it now.
All this time...
Doc Urbica, are you okay? Are you crying?
Crying? No, I just...
It's not important.
This isn't over, Sonny. Dorothy was never your enemy!
How long has it been since you were last here, Director Saria?
Hm?
Going a little spacey?
That's not like you... Thinking about the test site?
Do you remember Haydn 1 laboratory?
Of course I do. That's where Ifrit's from, right? We even investigated that place together.
I've been to several hundred similar ruins since.
It's very risky to work with the military. Rhine Lab hides behind layers of contracts and conducts these dangerous experiments with other laboratories doing the dirty work.
But this time, Ferdinand chose to charge into the fray himself.
That tells us Rhine is one careless move away from turning into one of those ruins.
If he fails... Ferdinand will end up buried under the ruins too, right? His real cause of death won't even hit the papers.
Ferdinand has no way out of this anymore. He has no choice but to make sure this experiment succeeds.
Nngh...
Are you worried about Silence and the gang?
I'm only analyzing the risks.
Huh, I thought you were more honest than that.
Okay, okay. She'll be fine. She's a lot stronger now than she used to be, just to look out for Iffy.
Are you consoling me, Director Muelsyse?
That's unnecessary.
I've never doubted Silence's and Ptilopsis's abilities. They will assume their own responsibilities, and so will we.

opts: We will work with each other.
I believe in our operators.
You're showing more of a human touch, Saria.

lines.x: Did I ever mention that I've always really admired the way Rhodes Island works?
My, you really have a way with words. Makes me want to jump ship and hop over.
Right, right? You feel the same.
I knew I wasn't mistaken.
Multiple power armor signals approaching.
Doctor, the Liberi merc is catching up to us.
Perhaps she's been waiting nearby.
Dr. Doctor, are you sure Kristen is here?
Saria's right. Ferdinand can't take this failure, but we don't have time for trial and error, do we?

opts: Ferdinand doesn't want to let anyone find Control.
There's only one place that's in his full control.
There's only one place to hide from prying eyes.

lines.x: Rhine Lab headquarters.
So that's the place that checks the most boxes, huh?
After I lost contact with Kristen, I went to her home, the test sites, and all her favorite spots...
Everyone who works at Rhine knows Control doesn't like to hang around the office.
Every day, a bunch of staff members ask where she is, but who knew that she could be right there under our noses?
Scan complete.
Doctor, it's just as you thought. There is a large group of armed militants guarding all exits on all floors.
Rhine HQ must already be under Ferdinand's control.
We need to make our moves carefully, and I'm including both Muelsyse and myself.
Seize them.
Dammit...
What are you blanking for?
All units, we've got a situation at the labs' entrance. It's a Pioneer and a Rhine researcher... Mrgh!
In the end, we still gotta hurt people.
That's self defense. I'll testify for you.
There's an emergency exit to the left. Run that way! We'll talk after we make it out—
Someone's coming!
Get ready!
Wait, you again?!
M-Miss Elena...
I didn't hurt you, did I?
I'm fine. I figured you'd strike, so I braced myself.
All of you, stay right where you are.
You're...
Mary Banner of the Trimounts City Police Department.
We're taking a kidnapping and assault suspect into custody. I'd appreciate your cooperation.
Suspect?
Mary...
You're a hard one to catch. Isn't that right, 'Team Leader?'
Drop your weapons and put your hands up.
Right hand too.
Put your right hand up, please.
A syringe?
Hold on, what are you trying to do?
This is a physical examination.
There might be a hazardous material leakage in these laboratories. Regulations demand we execute prevention procedures on all personnel.
May I take a look at the medicine you're administering?
The situation is critical. Please don't get in the way.
Warning: Procedure does not match existing records.
What you are doing is completely against the rules...
Rules? I don't need a researcher who was... kicked out of Rhine to teach me about rules.
Hmm...
What about me, then?
Do I have the right to ask you to turn this syringe over?
Director Franks...
I am the overseer of Site 359. State your objectives immediately.
Sorry, Director Franks, but Director Clooney has taken over responsibility for all your site's functions.
Without his orders, you can't ask us about our operation.
If I recall correctly, only Control can change an experiment's overseer.
What gives Ferdinand the right to overstep his bounds?
I can't answer that.
This should be enough...
Mission complete. All units, pull out.
They're gone...?
Affirmative. The Defense Section personnel are quickly evacuating the site.
I thought they were here to take the Pioneers away... Am I mistaken about Ferdinand?
Ferdinand!
Ugh...!
Dr. Silence, all Pioneers who received injections have lost consciousness.
Tend to them immediately!
Director Franks, do you know how to administer emergency treatment... Director Franks?
Ma'am, I thought we were here to save Mr. Sonny...
Don't think you can change my mind with a couple of sentimental words!
You got my letter...
Trying to win my sympathy only works once, Romano.
I'm just glad I didn't let you get away. Otherwise, who knows what kind of surprises I'd have waiting for me?
You don't trust me...
Why should I?!
Four years ago, you didn't want to go to the quarantine zone. I trusted you, and helped you lay low—
Then how did you turn around and thank me?
I was outta my mind! My Oripathy plus my insurance premiums drove me nuts. I had no other...
You broke into my place and forced me to give you everything worth half a dime—Just for your goddamned insurance premium! You thought they'd let you back into your law firm if you paid it!
You know how much that scared Nana? Did you know she's been bedridden ever since? Remember how she doted on you, even more than she did for her own granddaughter?!
And how about this? After you got spooked and ran off, I was so worried the cops would find you, do you know what I went through to cover it all up for you?
You don't know anything.
You're a cold-blooded, selfish criminal. I should never have been so soft on you...
Officer!
It doesn't matter what kind of bad blood you two have with each other. We need to get out of here, otherwise—
He kidnapped you, and you're making his case for him?
Listen to me. The real problem is with Rhine Lab...
This shaking...
Uh... Is that coming from inside?
Dorothy's lab...
Run!!!
It... actually happened...
Such intense tremors... occurring across a large area... Is it a Catastrophe?
No, not a Catastrophe. It's the lab...
In the center of the laboratory cluster, a fissure appears on what should have been an exceedingly sturdy building.
It wasn't caused by the tremors.
The building is being torn apart from its deepest parts.
Something is breaking out of its shell, bit by bit, and unbelievably quickly.
In just an instant, half its floors are engulfed.
Above the rubble, the silver object starts bringing the entire site into its embrace.
The newly-born, colossal object gracefully twists its geometric figure and coldly stares down at the people on the ground.
None of this is beyond her comprehension.
She understands the principles behind all this, and she's calculated all the possibilities.
Yet she still forgets to breathe and blink, and she forgets all about the trembling earth beneath her feet and the roaring gusts all around.
Her dream has evolved from a blueprint and a word of promise to a reality.
How beautiful...
But also terrifying.
Get down—
You were in tremendous danger... If you were just one inch closer, you would've been crushed by the bricks!
It's too late...
What is?
Doctor, you failed to stop me. My experiment... succeeded.
Director Clooney! What's happening at the laboratories—
You are frightened.
Frightened...? Haha, of course not. I'm just overwhelmed with emotion at these results! I... I'm truly happy for you and Director Franks.
Your teeth are chattering.
Ha... haha...
Perfect.
Fearing such great unknowns is an instinct of the weak.
You're right. Before your results, our enemies... the Columbia's enemies will tremble in fear!
You're talking about terrorizing our enemies?
That's what the army men worry about all day and night. They give me the funding, and I give them the weapons—We scratch each other's backs, and that's where it ends.
But you did remind me...
It's time to move to the next phase.
Wouldn't that provoke it...? What about the people at the site?
They're making the greatest sacrifice of all.
Remember their names.
As pioneers in the truest sense, their names will go down in history.
Their fearlessness gave birth to this ferocious beast, and we will take control of it and make it ours. It will be our hands and eyes.
Just as our ancestors, the brave settlers, brought burdenbeasts under their control, enabling civilization to conquer the wilderness.
We, too, will conquer this beast created by technology and expand mankind's reach into even greater unknowns.
—We will be the trailblazers who expand the frontiers of civilization.
Are... are these the silver monsters? How did they grow so big...?
We'll need something special to do any damage!
You goddamned—monster!
Did you see those arrows?
The three arrows, all shot at different times, approach the silver geometric figure in a tidy row. The farther they fly, the slower they get, all the way down to an immeasurable crawl.
Yet they remain suspended in the air.
Enveloped by the silver light, they disappear from sight at the very same moment.
So the monster's got some magic tricks?
It disintegrated them.
But it seems awfully quiet to me...
Watch out! Move!
Urgh... Was that an energy beam, or some crossbow bolts? Where did it come from...? It was so quick, I couldn't catch it.
Logic can't explain its movements and attack patterns.
Perhaps... to that thing, moving and attacking are one and the same...
Supposing this really is the... final product of Dorothy's experiment, then the whole site is within its range of perception.
